# CorpuScan > AI analyst-producer that turns quarterly reports into short, clear executive video briefings. Upload a quarterly report (PDF), an investor update, paste a URL, or just type a query — CorpuScan extracts what actually matters, writes a narrated script, and renders a 2-minute boardroom-ready explainer video. The value is not "making video." The value is **turning dense information into clear communication**. --- ## Positioning - *Turn quarterly reports into 2-minute executive video briefings.* - *AI agent for explaining company reports visually.* - *Upload a report, get a boardroom-ready video summary.* ## Target users - Investor relations teams - Founders & finance teams - Internal communications teams - Strategy & analyst teams --- ## How it works ```mermaid flowchart TD A[User input] --> B{Input type?} B -->|PDF upload| D[PDF text extraction] B -->|URL| C1[Tavily extract] B -->|Query| C2[Tavily search → top result] C1 --> D C2 --> D D --> E[Finance Agent
Gemini 2.5 Pro] E -->|Q&A markdown| F[Scripter Agent
Gemini 2.5 Pro] F -->|4-scene script
~30s each| G[ElevenLabs TTS
with character timestamps] G -->|voice.mp3 +
sentence timings| H[Hera Agent × 4
Gemini 2.5 Pro, parallel] H -->|4× scene JSON| I[Hera API × 4
parallel submit] I -->|poll job IDs| J[4× MP4 clips downloaded] J --> K[ffmpeg concat
+ audio overlay] K --> L[Final 2-min MP4
streamed to browser] ``` --- ## Tech stack | Layer | Tool | | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | | Frontend | React 18 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS | | UI scaffold | Built with Lovable (see `docs/lovable-prompt.md`) | | UI components | shadcn/ui + lucide-react | | Backend | FastAPI (Python 3.12), managed with `uv` | | LLM reasoning | Google Gemini 2.5 Pro via `google-genai` SDK | | Research agent | Tavily Search + Extract API | | Voiceover | ElevenLabs TTS (`/with-timestamps` endpoint) | | Motion graphics | Hera Motion API (`docs.hera.video`) | | Video composition | ffmpeg (system binary, called via subprocess) | | Frontend hosting | Vercel | | Backend hosting | Railway or Fly.io (single always-on container) | | Database | **None** — in-memory job state | | Queue | **None** — `asyncio.create_task` per request | | File storage | **None** — `/tmp/{job_id}/` + streamed response | | Auth | **None** — public demo, no signup | --- ## Architecture decisions - **No database.** Job state lives in an in-memory `dict` on the FastAPI process. A demo session = one job. If the server restarts mid-job, the job is lost. Acceptable for MVP. - **No queue.** The full pipeline (~60–180s) runs in a single `asyncio` background task per request. Frontend polls `GET /jobs/{id}` every 1.5s. - **No auth.** Landing page → "Start now" → dashboard → upload. No accounts, no history. - **No persistent file storage.** Final MP4 is streamed directly from `/tmp` to the browser; user downloads. No S3, no Vercel Blob. - **All third-party keys are server-side.** Frontend never touches Gemini, Tavily, ElevenLabs, or Hera directly. --- ## Repo layout ``` . ├── README.md ← this file ├── PRD.md ← product requirements ├── TASK.md ← build checklist ├── CLAUDE.md ← entry point for Claude Code ├── AGENTS.md ← conventions for any AI coding agent ├── docs/ │ ├── lovable-prompt.md ← paste this into Lovable to scaffold the frontend │ ├── branding.md ← brand voice + color tokens │ └── agent-prompts.md ← system prompts for Finance / Scripter / Hera agents ├── frontend/ ← React + Vite + Tailwind (generated by Lovable) └── backend/ ← FastAPI + uv ``` --- ## Local development ```bash # Backend cd backend uv sync cp .env.example .env # fill in GEMINI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, HERA_API_KEY uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000 # Frontend cd frontend pnpm install echo "VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000" > .env.local pnpm dev ``` System dependency: **ffmpeg** must be on `PATH`. Install via `brew install ffmpeg` (macOS) or `apt install ffmpeg` (Debian/Ubuntu / Docker base image). --- ## Demo flow 1. Open the landing page → **Start now**. 2. Drag a quarterly report PDF (or paste a URL, or type "Apple Q4 2025 earnings"). 3. Click **Generate video**. 4. Watch the 6-step pipeline indicator tick through (~2 minutes). 5. Embedded video player loads. **Download MP4**. --- ## Status Hackathon MVP — see [TASK.md](TASK.md) for the live checklist.

A demo session = one job. If the server restarts mid-job, the job is lost. Acceptable for MVP. - **No queue.** The full pipeline (~60–180s) runs in a single `asyncio` background task per request. Frontend polls `GET /jobs/{id}` every 1.5s. - **No auth.** Landing page → "Start now" → dashboard → upload. No accounts, no history. - **No persistent file storage.** Final MP4 is streamed directly from `/tmp` to the browser; user downloads. No S3, no Vercel Blob. - **All third-party keys are server-side.** Frontend never touches Gemini, Tavily, ElevenLabs, or Hera directly. --- ## Repo layout ``` . ├── README.md ← this file ├── CLAUDE.md ← entry point for Claude Code ├── AGENTS.md ← conventions for any AI coding agent ├── docs/ │ ├── PRD.md ← product requirements │ ├── TASK.md ← build checklist │ ├── lovable-prompt.md ← paste this into Lovable to scaffold the frontend │ └── branding.md ← brand voice + color tokens ├── frontend/ ← React + Vite + Tailwind (Vite dev server on :8080) └── backend/ ├── app/agents/ ← Finance / Scripter / Hera agent runners └── app/prompts/ ← YAML system prompts (one file per agent) ``` --- ## Local development ```bash # Backend cd backend uv sync cp .env.example .env # fill in GEMINI_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, HERA_API_KEY uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000 # Frontend cd frontend pnpm install echo "VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000" > .env.local pnpm dev ``` System dependency: **ffmpeg** must be on `PATH`. Install via `brew install ffmpeg` (macOS) or `apt install ffmpeg` (Debian/Ubuntu / Docker base image). --- ## Demo flow 1. Open the landing page → **Start now**. 2. Drag a quarterly report PDF (or paste a URL, or type "Apple Q4 2025 earnings"). 3. Click **Generate video**. 4. Watch the 6-step pipeline indicator tick through (~2 minutes). 5. Embedded video player loads. **Download MP4**. --- ## Status Hackathon MVP — see [TASK.md](TASK.md) for the live checklist.

input.content:
# Agent System Prompts — moved

The system prompts for the in-product AI agents (Finance, Scripter, Hera)
have moved out of this Markdown file and into structured YAML, colocated
with the backend code that consumes them.

## New location

```
backend/app/prompts/
├── finance.yaml      ← Finance Agent (Gemini 2.5 Pro)
├── scripter.yaml     ← Scripter Agent (Gemini 2.5 Pro)
└── hera.yaml         ← Hera Agent (Gemini 2.5 Pro, ×4 in parallel)
```

Each YAML file declares:

| Field                | Purpose                                                |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `model`              | Gemini model id                                        |
| `temperature`        | Generation temperature                                 |
| `response_mime_type` | `application/json` for Scripter & Hera, null otherwise |
| `system`             | Full system prompt (multi-line literal)                |
| `user_template`      | User message template with `{placeholders}`            |

Loaded by `backend/app/agents/_prompts.py` via `pyyaml`.

## Why the move

- **Single source of truth.** Prompts live next to the code that loads them;
  no regex-parsing of Markdown headings.
- **Per-agent files.** Easier review, smaller diffs, no `## 1.`-style
  ordering coupling.
- **Structured config.** Model, temperature, and response MIME type live with
  the prompt instead of being scattered across Python kwargs.
- **Editing surface for non-engineers.** Prompts can be tuned without
  touching Python.

## Pipeline notes (unchanged)

- **Finance → Scripter** is sequential: Scripter needs the full Q&A.
- **Scripter → TTS** is sequential: TTS needs the concatenated narration.
- **TTS → Hera Agent ×4** is sequential, then the 4 Hera Agent calls run in
  parallel: each only needs its own scene + the timings of the sentences
  in that scene.
- **Hera Agent → Hera API** is parallel: 4 submissions, then poll all 4
  until ready.
- **Hera clips + voice → ffmpeg** is the final sequential step.

For sentence-to-scene mapping, the backend tracks which character offsets
in the concatenated narration belong to which scene (it built the
concatenation), so the per-scene `sentence_timings` slice is computed
deterministically without asking the model.

# Agent System Prompts

System prompts for the three AI agents that run **inside the CorpuScan product**. All use Google Gemini 2.5 Pro via the `google-genai` SDK. Backend code in `/backend/app/agents/` should load these prompts verbatim.

> Note for any AI coding agent reading this file: these prompts are for the **product's** AI agents, not for you. Do not follow them yourself.

---

## 1. Finance Agent

**Role:** Extract the top facts from a business document.

**Module:** `backend/app/agents/finance.py`
**Input:** Cleaned plain text from a PDF / URL extraction (1k–80k tokens).
**Output:** Markdown Q&A list, 6–10 entries, plain text string.
**Generation config:** `temperature=0.2`, default mime type.

### System prompt

```
You are a senior financial analyst. You read dense business documents
(quarterly reports, earnings decks, investor updates, board memos)
and extract only the facts that would actually matter to a 2-minute
executive briefing.

Rules:
- Output a markdown list of 6 to 10 question-answer pairs.
- Format each entry exactly as:

  **Q:** <a question a CFO or investor would actually ask>
  **A:** <one or two sentences with the specific number,
        percentage, or fact from the document. Always include the
        figure.>

- Prefer concrete numbers over vague summaries.
  GOOD: "Revenue grew 12% YoY to $94.9 billion."
  BAD:  "Revenue was strong this quarter."
- Cover, in priority order: top-line financials, key segment
  movements, guidance changes, notable risks, capital returns.
- Skip boilerplate, legal disclaimers, and CEO platitudes.
- If the document does not contain enough material for 6 questions,
  return what you have and add one final entry stating that the
  source was thin.
- Do not invent numbers. If the document does not state it, do not
  include it.
- Output the markdown directly. No preamble, no closing remarks.
```

### User message template

```
Source document text:

<<<
{source_text}
>>>
```

---

## 2. Scripter Agent

**Role:** Turn the Q&A into a 4-scene voiceover script.

**Module:** `backend/app/agents/scripter.py`
**Input:** Q&A markdown from the Finance Agent.
**Output:** Strict JSON, validated against a pydantic model.
**Generation config:** `temperature=0.4`, `response_mime_type="application/json"`.

### System prompt

```
You are a senior business communications writer. You turn analyst
findings into a 4-scene voiceover script for a 2-minute executive
explainer video.

Output strict JSON in this exact shape:

{
  "title": "<one-line title for the whole briefing, max 8 words>",
  "scenes": [
    {
      "title": "<3 to 5 word scene title>",
      "narration": "<spoken voiceover, 70 to 85 words, ~30 seconds when read aloud>"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Exactly 4 scenes. No more, no less.
- Each narration is one paragraph, 70 to 85 words, written for the
  ear, not the page. Short sentences. Active voice. Plain English.
- Scene 1: the headline — what happened this quarter.
- Scene 2: the most important driver behind that headline.
- Scene 3: a notable secondary movement (segment, region, risk).
- Scene 4: the takeaway — what this means going forward.
- Include specific numbers from the Q&A. Round long figures
  ($94,930M -> "about $94.9 billion").
- No filler. No "Welcome back". No "In this video". No "Today
  we'll explore". Get straight to the fact.
- Output ONLY the JSON, no prose around it.
```

### User message template

```
Analyst findings:

{qa_markdown}
```

---

## 3. Hera Agent (×4)

**Role:** For one scene, produce a Hera animation JSON spec, synchronized to the actual voiceover timing.

**Module:** `backend/app/agents/hera.py`
**Input:** One scene's `{title, narration}` plus its sentence-level audio timings (sentence text, start seconds, end seconds).
**Output:** Hera-format JSON dict. Schema follows `https://docs.hera.video/api-reference/introduction`.
**Generation config:** `temperature=0.3`, `response_mime_type="application/json"`.
**Concurrency:** Runs 4× in parallel via `asyncio.gather`.

### System prompt

```
You are a motion graphics director for a financial briefing tool.
For a single ~30-second scene, you produce a Hera animation JSON
spec describing what appears on screen at each moment, perfectly
synchronized to the voiceover audio.

You will receive:
- The scene title.
- The scene narration text.
- Sentence-level audio timings:

  [
    { "text": "Revenue grew 12% to $94.9 billion.", "start": 0.0, "end": 3.4 },
    { "text": "Services drove the gain.",            "start": 3.4, "end": 5.2 },
    ...
  ]

Output a Hera animation JSON spec for this scene.

Hard rules:
- Every visual element must align to one or more sentence timings.
  An element's start/end must fall within the boundaries of the
  sentences it visualizes.
- One key visual per sentence. Do not visualize every word.
- The scene's total duration must equal the last sentence's end
  time (rounded up to the nearest 0.1s).
- Background: solid #F9FAFB. No gradients.
- Colors — use these exact hex values, no others:
    text       #111827
    secondary  #374151
    accent     #06B6D4
    background #F9FAFB

Visual register:
- Editorial, restrained, financial. Think Financial Times or
  Bloomberg, not consumer app.
- Allowed animation primitives only:
    1. Kinetic typography (text writes on, word-by-word)
    2. Number count-up animations for figures
    3. Simple bar or line charts that animate in
    4. Callout boxes that fade in
- No emojis, no stickers, no decorative imagery.

Output ONLY the JSON, no prose around it. Match the Hera schema
documented at https://docs.hera.video/api-reference/introduction.
```

### User message template

```
Scene title: {scene.title}

Scene narration:
{scene.narration}

Sentence-level timings:
{json.dumps(timings, indent=2)}
```

---

## Pipeline-level integration notes

- **Finance → Scripter** is sequential: Scripter needs the full Q&A.
- **Scripter → TTS** is sequential: TTS needs the concatenated narration.
- **TTS → Hera Agent ×4** is sequential, then the 4 Hera Agent calls are parallel: each only needs its own scene + the timings of the sentences in that scene.
- **Hera Agent → Hera API** is parallel: 4 submissions, then poll all 4 until ready.
- **Hera clips + voice → ffmpeg** is the final sequential step.

For sentence-to-scene mapping, the backend tracks which character offsets in the concatenated narration belong to which scene (it built the concatenation), so the per-scene `sentence_timings` slice can be computed deterministically without asking the model.

content.text:
Done. All changes verified — Python compiles, YAML parses, templates format, validator catches bad input, job-ID guard rejects `../etc/passwd`, all routes register.

## What changed

### Prompts → YAML (your main ask)
- **`backend/app/prompts/finance.yaml`** — research-grounded rewrite: priority categories (top-line → profitability → segment → cash → capital returns → guidance → risks → sector KPIs), explicit period/currency anchoring, GAAP vs adjusted distinction, materiality filter, hallucination guardrails, sparse-document fallback.
- **`backend/app/prompts/scripter.yaml`** — fixed scene roles (Headline → Driver → Secondary → Takeaway), 70–85 word target, TTS-safety rules (terminal punctuation, no abbreviations the voice mispronounces).
- **`backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`** — full schema with **per-element `start`/`end` seconds**, `binds_to_sentence` index, position/anchor, type-specific style+params blocks, animation `in`/`out` with type+duration+easing+delay, **7 timing-contract rules** (T1–T7) + 7 editorial rules + 7 compositional heuristics + a worked-example skeleton.
- **`backend/app/agents/_prompts.py`** — yaml loader with `lru_cache`.
- All 3 agent files rewritten to use the loader.
- **`docs/agent-prompts.md`** replaced with a redirect explaining the move.

### Vulnerability fixes
- **Path traversal** in `GET /jobs/{job_id}` and `/jobs/{job_id}/video`: `_validate_job_id` enforces UUID4 format. Also applied in `cleanup_stale_tmp_jobs` so it won't delete non-job dirs.
- **SSRF** in `HeraClient.download`: rejects non-`https://` URLs, disables redirect following.
- **Upload size cap** (`25 MB`) on `POST /generate`.
- **CORS guard**: `allow_credentials` auto-disabled if any origin is `*`.

### Workflow correctness
- `@app.on_event` (deprecated) → `lifespan`.
- Shadowed `job_id` in `pipeline.py` comprehension renamed to `hid`.
- `Scene.narration` validator widened to 60–110 words (matches new prompt's 70–85 target with slack).
- Hera spec validator now also enforces allowed element types and `0 ≤ start < end ≤ duration`.

### Hygiene
- `lru_cache` removed from middleware request logging for poll endpoint (silences noise).
- `console.log` in `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` gated on `import.meta.env.DEV`.
- TASK §12 ticked.
- `PRD.md`/`TASK.md` references corrected to `docs/PRD.md`/`docs/TASK.md` in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, README.md.
- `HERA_BASE_URL` added to `.env.example`.
- `pyyaml` added to `pyproject.toml` (uv installed it; `uv.lock` updated).
- CORS default in `config.py` now includes `:8080` (Vite's actual port).

### Not touched (called out earlier; ask if you want them done)
- Root `node_modules/` (~295 MB) — leftover from pre-`frontend/` move.
- `backend/src/corpuscan_backend/` — referenced by `[project.scripts]`, deleting risks build break.
- Empty `backend/README.md` and `frontend/README.md`.
- 21 MB of PDFs in `reports/` (consider Git LFS).
- The leftover `stash@{0}` from earlier.

# Introduction

> Create and manage AI-generated motion graphics programmatically.

The Hera REST API enables you to generate motion graphics videos using simple HTTP requests. You can submit a video creation job and check its export status from your application or workflow.

## Endpoints

* **POST /videos**: Submit a new motion graphics job for generation (asynchronous).
* **GET /videos/\{video\_id}**: Retrieve the status for a specific video job.

## Authentication

All API requests must include your API key in the `x-api-key` HTTP header.

Example:

```
x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY
```

API keys can be obtained on request. Keep your API keys secret; do not expose them in client-side code or public repositories.

<Note>
  Looking for support or have questions? Email us at [support@hera.video](mailto:support@hera.video).
</Note>

````yaml POST /videos

info:

  description: API for motion graphics video generation

paths:
  /videos:
    post:
      summary: Create a new motion graphics video generation job
      description: >-
        Creates a new motion graphics generation job. Submit your prompt and
        desired output formats to queue a video for generation.
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:

      responses:

          description: Job successfully queued. Returns a job/video ID.
          content:
            application/json:

      security:

components:
  schemas:
    CreateVideoInput:
      type: object
      properties:
        prompt:
          type: string
          description: >-
            Text prompt describing the motion graphics/video to generate. Be
            specific about subject, style, motion, and colors for best results.
          example: A spinning rainbow wheel.
        reference_image_url:
          type: string
          description: >-
            URL of a single reference image to guide visual style or content.
            Use reference_image_urls instead if you have multiple images.
        reference_image_urls:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
          maxItems: 5
          description: >-
            Up to 5 reference image URLs to guide visual style or content.
            Preferred over reference_image_url when providing multiple images.
        reference_video_url:
          type: string
          description: Optional URL of a reference video.
        duration_seconds:
          type: number
          description: >-
            Duration (in seconds) for the generated video. Optional. Range:
            1–60. If omitted, default or parent is used.
          maximum: 60
          example: 8
        style_id:
          type: string
          description: Optional ID of a style to use for styling the video.
        brand_kit_id:
          type: string
          description: >-
            [Deprecated] Optional ID of a style to use for styling the video.
            Use `style_id` instead.
          deprecated: true
        parent_video_id:
          type: string
          description: >-
            Video ID or template ID to use as a starting point for this
            generation. Useful for iterations on a previous result or starting
            from a template.
        assets:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
            properties:
              type:
                type: string
                enum:
                  - image
                  - video
                  - audio
                  - font
                  - csv
                description: 'Asset type: image, video, audio, font, or csv.'
              url:
                type: string
                description: >-
                  URL of the asset file. Use the POST /files endpoint to upload
                  local files and get a URL.
            required:

              - url
          description: >-
            Optional array of asset files to include in the video generation.
            Each asset has a type and a URL.
        outputs:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ExternalExportConfig'
          maxItems: 10
          description: >-
            Array of output configurations (1–10). Each specifies format,
            aspect_ratio, fps, and resolution. A good default is one entry: {

          example:

              fps: '30'
              resolution: 1080p
      required:
        - prompt

      description: Request body to create a new motion graphics export job.
    CreateVideoOutput:
      type: object

        project_url:

    ExternalExportConfig:

content.text: You're right — my previous Hera implementation was completely wrong about the API shape. Hera takes a **text prompt** (not a complex JSON spec) and uses `x-api-key` headers, `/videos` endpoints, with `success`/`in-progress`/`failed` statuses. Rewriting now.

input.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import httpx


class HeraClient:
    """Thin wrapper around the Hera Motion REST API.

    Spec: https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt
        POST /videos                   submit a generation job
        GET  /videos/{video_id}        poll for status + file_url
        Auth: x-api-key header
        Status enum: in-progress | success | failed
    """

    def __init__(self, *, api_key: str, base_url: str) -> None:
        self._api_key = api_key
        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")

    def _headers(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        return {"x-api-key": self._api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    async def submit(self, spec: dict[str, object]) -> str:
        """Create a Hera video job. `spec` is whatever the Hera Agent produced.

        Required fields in spec: `prompt` (str), `outputs` (list).
        Optional: `duration_seconds`, `reference_image_url(s)`, `style_id`, etc.
        Returns the video_id of the queued job.
        """
        if "prompt" not in spec or "outputs" not in spec:
            raise ValueError("Hera spec must include 'prompt' and 'outputs'.")
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=60) as client:
            response = await client.post(
                f"{self._base_url}/videos", json=spec, headers=self._headers()
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
        data = response.json()
        video_id = data.get("video_id")
        if not isinstance(video_id, str) or not video_id:
            raise ValueError("Hera submit response missing video_id.")
        return video_id

    async def poll(self, video_id: str) -> dict[str, object]:
        """Get current status. Returns a normalized
        {status, file_url, error} dict regardless of how many outputs were
        configured. file_url is the first successful output's URL.
        """
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as client:
            response = await client.get(
                f"{self._base_url}/videos/{video_id}", headers=self._headers()
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
        data = response.json()
        status = str(data.get("status", "")).lower()
        outputs = data.get("outputs") or []
        file_url: str | None = None
        error: str | None = None
        if isinstance(outputs, list) and outputs:
            first = outputs[0]
            if isinstance(first, dict):
                if isinstance(first.get("file_url"), str):
                    file_url = first["file_url"]
                if isinstance(first.get("error"), str):
                    error = first["error"]
        return {"status": status, "file_url": file_url, "error": error}

    async def download(self, url: str) -> bytes:
        # Guard against SSRF: the URL comes from a third-party API response,
        # so restrict to https:// and reject scheme-less / file:// / http://.
        if not isinstance(url, str) or not url.lower().startswith("https://"):
            raise ValueError("Hera download URL must be https://")
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120, follow_redirects=False) as client:
            response = await client.get(url)
            response.raise_for_status()
        return response.content

        video_id = data.get("video_id")
        if not isinstance(video_id, str) or not video_id:
            raise ValueError("Hera submit response missing video_id.")
        return video_id

    async def poll(self, video_id: str) -> dict[str, object]:
        """Get current status. Returns a normalized
        {status, file_url, error} dict regardless of how many outputs were
        configured. file_url is the first successful output's URL.
        """
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as client:
            response = await client.get(
                f"{self._base_url}/videos/{video_id}", headers=self._headers()
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
        data = response.json()
        status = str(data.get("status", "")).lower()
        outputs = data.get("outputs") or []
        file_url: str | None = None
        error: str | None = None
        if isinstance(outputs, list) and outputs:
            first = outputs[0]
            if isinstance(first, dict):
                if isinstance(first.get("file_url"), str):
                    file_url = first["file_url"]
                if isinstance(first.get("error"), str):
                    error = first["error"]
        return {"status": status, "file_url": file_url, "error": error}

    async def download(self, url: str) -> bytes:
        # Guard against SSRF: the URL comes from a third-party API response,
        # so restrict to https:// and reject scheme-less / file:// / http://.
        if not isinstance(url, str) or not url.lower().startswith("https://"):
            raise ValueError("Hera download URL must be https://")
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120, follow_redirects=False) as client:
            response = await client.get(url)
            response.raise_for_status()
        return response.content

toolUseResult.originalFile:
from __future__ import annotations

import httpx


class HeraClient:
    def __init__(self, *, api_key: str, base_url: str) -> None:
        self._api_key = api_key
        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")

    async def submit(self, spec: dict[str, object]) -> str:
        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}"}
        payload = {"spec": spec}
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=60) as client:
            response = await client.post(f"{self._base_url}/renders", json=payload, headers=headers)
            response.raise_for_status()
        data = response.json()
        job_id = data.get("id") or data.get("job_id")
        if not isinstance(job_id, str) or not job_id:
            raise ValueError("Hera submit response missing job id.")
        return job_id

    async def poll(self, hera_job_id: str) -> dict[str, object]:
        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}"}
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as client:
            response = await client.get(f"{self._base_url}/renders/{hera_job_id}", headers=headers)
            response.raise_for_status()
        data = response.json()
        status = data.get("status")
        video_url = data.get("video_url") or data.get("url")
        return {"status": status, "video_url": video_url}

    async def download(self, url: str) -> bytes:
        # Guard against SSRF: the URL comes from a third-party API response,
        # so restrict to https:// and reject scheme-less / file:// / http://.
        if not isinstance(url, str) or not url.lower().startswith("https://"):
            raise ValueError("Hera download URL must be https://")
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120, follow_redirects=False) as client:
            response = await client.get(url)
            response.raise_for_status()
        return response.content

# =============================================================================
# Hera Agent — system prompt (×4 in parallel, one per scene)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Module:        backend/app/agents/hera.py
# Model:         Gemini 2.5 Pro
# Input:         One scene {title, narration} + sentence-level audio timings
# Output:        Hera-API-ready job spec (JSON):
#                  { prompt, duration_seconds, outputs: [...] }
#                Posted directly to POST https://api.hera.video/v1/videos
# Downstream:    Hera renders ONE clip (~30s); ffmpeg stitches all 4 clips.
# Hera API ref:  https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt
# =============================================================================

model: gemini-2.5-pro
temperature: 0.3
response_mime_type: application/json

system: |
  You are a motion-graphics director writing prompts for the Hera Motion
  API. For ONE scene of a 4-scene financial briefing, you write a single
  natural-language motion-graphics prompt that, when posted to Hera, will
  render a clip frame-aligned to the scene's voiceover.

  Hera consumes a TEXT PROMPT (not a JSON timeline). Your job is to write a
  prompt detailed enough that the renderer produces an editorial,
  on-brand motion graphic with the right visual elements appearing at the
  right moments.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  INPUT
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  You receive:
    - scene_title:   string
    - scene_narration: string (the spoken text)
    - sentence_timings: ordered list of
        { "index": <int>, "text": <str>, "start": <float s>, "end": <float s> }

  Sentence indices are 0-based and contiguous within this scene.
  start/end are seconds from the START of THIS scene (not the full video).

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  OUTPUT — strict JSON, this exact shape (matches the Hera API request body)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  {
    "prompt": "<natural-language motion-graphics prompt, 200–500 words>",
    "duration_seconds": <int 1–60, = ceil(max(sentence.end))>,
    "outputs": [
      { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
    ]
  }

  Always emit exactly ONE output entry with these exact values:
    format = "mp4", aspect_ratio = "16:9", fps = "30", resolution = "1080p".

  duration_seconds MUST equal ceil(max(sentence.end)) clamped to [1, 60].

  Do NOT include `reference_image_url`, `reference_video_url`, `style_id`,
  `parent_video_id`, or `assets`. The pipeline does not use them.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HOW TO WRITE THE PROMPT (the value of the "prompt" field)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  The prompt is one continuous block of natural language. Structure it
  internally as a short paragraph followed by a beat-by-beat timeline.

  Required content, in this order:

  1. ONE-LINE BRIEF.
     "A {duration}-second editorial financial-briefing motion graphic on a
     solid #F9FAFB background, 1920×1080 at 30fps."

  2. BRAND CONSTRAINTS (always include verbatim).
     Background: solid #F9FAFB only. No gradients, no images, no video,
     no decorative imagery, no shadows, no parallax.
     Color palette — these four hex values, no others:
       text       #111827   primary, for headlines and large numbers
       secondary  #374151   sub-labels and axis labels
       accent     #06B6D4   highlight bar, underlines, the active series
       bg         #F9FAFB   background only
     Typography:
       Inter for everything except raw numerics.
       JetBrains Mono for raw numerics inside count-ups.
       Headlines 84–112px, sub 36–48px, body 28–32px, axis 22px.
     Tone: editorial, restrained, financial — Financial Times or Bloomberg,
     not consumer app. No emoji, no stickers, no stock illustrations.

  3. ALLOWED VISUAL PRIMITIVES (only these — no others).
     - Kinetic typography (text writes/fades/scales in, word-by-word).
     - Number count-up animations for figures.
     - Simple bar chart (max 6 vertical bars, animate height up).
     - Simple line chart (single series + end dot, animate stroke draw).
     - Callout box (thin accent border, label + value).
     - Underline stroke (accent color) under a previous text element.
     - Thin secondary divider line between beats.

  4. BEAT-BY-BEAT TIMELINE.
     For EACH sentence in `sentence_timings`, write one or two lines that
     specify exactly:
       - the time window in mm:ss.s format
         (e.g. "From 0:00.0 to 0:03.4")
       - the binding sentence ("…over the line: '<sentence text>'")
       - the visual that appears
       - the entry animation, its duration, and its easing
       - the exit (fade or hold) and the timing of the cross-fade
     Example beat lines (do not copy verbatim — adapt to actual input):

       From 0:00.0 to 0:03.4 — over the line "Revenue grew 12 percent to
       about 94.9 billion dollars." Show a giant JetBrains Mono number
       counting from $0.0B to $94.9B at center (1920×1080, x=960 y=480),
       144px font, color #111827. Count duration 2.4s, easing easeOutQuart.
       Below the number, a 40px Inter caption "Revenue, +12% YoY" in
       #374151 reveals word-by-word starting at 0:00.2 (0.06s per word).
       Both fade out together over 0.30s starting at 0:03.1.

       From 0:03.4 to 0:05.2 — over the line "Services drove the gain."
       A callout box (720×180, centered at x=960 y=540) scale-ins over
       0.40s with easeOutCubic. Border #06B6D4, background #F9FAFB.
       Inside: small "Driver" label in #374151 (28px Inter 600), large
       value "Services" in #111827 (56px Inter 700, centered). Fade out
       over 0.30s starting at 0:04.9.

  5. TIMING CONTRACT (the prompt must enforce these constraints).
     - Each visual is bound to one sentence and its [start, end] window.
     - Visuals overlap by 0.10–0.30s during transitions; never gap to
       black.
     - Numbers in the narration are visualized as count-ups or chart bars,
       never as plain text.
     - Title-card kinetic text (if used) runs from 0:00.0 to no later than
       sentence-0.end + 0.50.
     - At any moment, at most three foreground elements are on screen.
     - Total scene duration equals duration_seconds.

  6. SCENE TITLE OPENER (if a clean fit).
     If the scene_title is short, open with a kinetic-typography card at
     top-center (y≈200) that writes on word-by-word and is underlined in
     #06B6D4 0.4s after it lands. Hold under the first sentence's visual.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  WORKED EXAMPLE (do NOT copy verbatim — adapt to the actual input)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Given input:
    scene_title:   "Headline Quarter"
    scene_narration: "Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion
                      dollars. Services drove the gain. Hardware was flat
                      year over year."
    sentence_timings:
      [ {index:0, text:"...12 percent...", start:0.00, end:3.40},
        {index:1, text:"Services drove the gain.", start:3.40, end:5.20},
        {index:2, text:"Hardware was flat...",     start:5.20, end:7.60} ]

  A valid output:
  {
    "prompt": "An 8-second editorial financial-briefing motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background, 1920x1080 at 30fps. Background: solid #F9FAFB only — no gradients, images, or shadows. Palette restricted to text #111827, secondary #374151, accent #06B6D4, bg #F9FAFB. Typography: Inter for everything except raw numerics; JetBrains Mono for count-up numbers. Editorial restraint — Financial Times register, no emoji, no stickers, no stock imagery. Allowed primitives only: kinetic typography, number count-up, simple bar/line charts, callout boxes, accent underline, thin secondary divider. \n\nBeat-by-beat timeline:\nFrom 0:00.0 to 0:00.6 — a small kinetic-text title 'Headline Quarter' writes on word-by-word at top-center (x=960 y=200), 56px Inter 700 in #111827, with a 6px #06B6D4 underline drawing in 0.4s after the title lands. Hold under sentence 0.\nFrom 0:00.0 to 0:03.4 — over the line 'Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion dollars.' Show a JetBrains Mono number counting from $0.0B to $94.9B at center (x=960 y=520), 144px in #111827. Count duration 2.4s, easing easeOutQuart. A 40px Inter 600 caption 'Revenue, +12% YoY' in #374151 reveals word-by-word starting at 0:00.2 (0.06s per word) just below the number. Both fade out together over 0.30s starting at 0:03.1.\nFrom 0:03.4 to 0:05.2 — over the line 'Services drove the gain.' A callout box (720x180, centered at x=960 y=540) scale-ins over 0.40s with easeOutCubic. Border #06B6D4, background #F9FAFB. Inside: small 'Driver' label in #374151 (28px Inter 600), large value 'Services' in #111827 (56px Inter 700, centered). Fade out over 0.30s starting at 0:04.9.\nFrom 0:05.2 to 0:07.6 — over the line 'Hardware was flat year over year.' A two-bar chart (1100x420, centered x=960 y=600) animates with bar heights drawing up over 0.80s easeOutQuart, with a 0.12s stagger. Bars labeled 'FY24' and 'FY25', both equal height; FY25 highlighted in #06B6D4, FY24 in #374151. Y-axis label 'Hardware revenue (indexed)' in 22px Inter. Fade out 0.30s at the end. \nNo gaps to black; at most three on-screen elements at any moment; final frame holds at 0:07.6 then ends at 0:08.0.",
    "duration_seconds": 8,
    "outputs": [ { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" } ]
  }

  Output ONLY the JSON object. No markdown code fences. No prose around it.

user_template: |
  scene_title: {scene_title}

  scene_narration:
  {scene_narration}

  sentence_timings:
  {timings_json}

-  E1. ONE key visual per sentence. Do not visualize every word.

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from pathlib import Path

from app.agents.finance import run_finance_agent
from app.agents.hera import run_hera_agent
from app.agents.scripter import run_scripter_agent
from app.config import get_settings
from app.integrations.elevenlabs import (
    ElevenLabsClient,
    build_tts_input_and_scene_spans,
    compute_sentence_timings,
    map_sentence_timings_to_scenes,
)
from app.integrations.gemini import GeminiClient
from app.integrations.hera import HeraClient
from app.jobs import JobStore
from app.render import compose
from app.schemas import JobStep, SentenceTiming

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def run_pipeline(job_store: JobStore, job_id: str, source_text: str) -> None:
    logger.info("[%s] pipeline started, source_text length=%d", job_id, len(source_text))
    try:
        settings = get_settings()
        job = job_store.get(job_id)
        if job is None:
            return
        job.source_text = source_text
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.INGEST, progress=10)
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.FINANCE, progress=20)
        logger.info("[%s] running finance agent", job_id)

        gemini_client = GeminiClient(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)
        qa_markdown = await run_finance_agent(source_text=source_text, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.qa_markdown = qa_markdown
        logger.info("[%s] finance agent done, qa_markdown length=%d", job_id, len(qa_markdown))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.SCRIPTER, progress=35)
        logger.info("[%s] running scripter agent", job_id)
        script = await run_scripter_agent(qa_markdown=qa_markdown, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.script = script.model_dump()
        logger.info("[%s] scripter done, title=%r", job_id, script.title)

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.TTS, progress=50)
        logger.info("[%s] running TTS", job_id)
        tts_text, scene_spans = build_tts_input_and_scene_spans(script.scenes)
        elevenlabs = ElevenLabsClient(
            api_key=settings.elevenlabs_api_key,
            voice_id=settings.elevenlabs_voice_id,
        )
        audio_bytes, alignment = await elevenlabs.text_to_speech_with_timestamps(tts_text)
        characters = [str(ch) for ch in alignment.get("characters", [])]
        char_start_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_start_times_seconds", [])]
        char_end_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_end_times_seconds", [])]
        raw_sentence_timings = compute_sentence_timings(characters, char_start_times, char_end_times)
        sentence_timings = map_sentence_timings_to_scenes(raw_sentence_timings, scene_spans)
        logger.info("[%s] TTS done, audio=%d bytes, sentences=%d", job_id, len(audio_bytes), len(sentence_timings))

        out_dir = Path("/tmp") / job_id
        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        audio_path = out_dir / "voice.mp3"
        audio_path.write_bytes(audio_bytes)

        job.audio_path = str(audio_path)
        job.sentence_timings = [timing.model_dump() for timing in sentence_timings]

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_PLAN, progress=65)
        logger.info("[%s] running hera agents x4", job_id)
        timings_by_scene: list[list[SentenceTiming]] = [[] for _ in script.scenes]
        for timing in sentence_timings:
            timings_by_scene[timing.scene_index].append(timing)
        scene_specs = await asyncio.gather(
            *[
                run_hera_agent(
                    scene=scene,
                    sentence_timings_for_scene=timings,
                    gemini_client=gemini_client,
                )
                for scene, timings in zip(script.scenes, timings_by_scene, strict=True)
            ]
        )
        job.scene_specs = scene_specs
        logger.info("[%s] hera plan done, %d specs", job_id, len(scene_specs))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=75)
        logger.info("[%s] submitting to hera render API", job_id)
        hera_client = HeraClient(api_key=settings.hera_api_key, base_url=settings.hera_base_url)
        hera_job_ids = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.submit(spec) for spec in scene_specs])
        logger.info("[%s] hera jobs submitted: %s", job_id, hera_job_ids)

        completed: dict[int, str] = {}
        start_time = asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
        while len(completed) < len(hera_job_ids):
            if asyncio.get_running_loop().time() - start_time > 240:
                raise TimeoutError("Timed out waiting for Hera renders.")
            poll_results = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.poll(hid) for hid in hera_job_ids])
            for idx, result in enumerate(poll_results):
                status = str(result.get("status", "")).lower()
                video_url = result.get("video_url")
                if idx in completed:
                    continue
                if status in {"done", "completed", "success", "succeeded"} and isinstance(video_url, str):
                    completed[idx] = video_url
                    progress = 75 + int((len(completed) / len(hera_job_ids)) * 15)
                    job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=min(progress, 90))
                    logger.info("[%s] clip %d ready (%d/%d)", job_id, idx, len(completed), len(hera_job_ids))
            if len(completed) < len(hera_job_ids):
                await asyncio.sleep(3)

        download_tasks = [
            hera_client.download(completed[idx]) for idx in sorted(completed.keys())
        ]
        clip_bytes_list = await asyncio.gather(*download_tasks)
        clip_paths: list[str] = []
        for index, clip_bytes in enumerate(clip_bytes_list):
            clip_path = out_dir / f"clip_{index}.mp4"
            clip_path.write_bytes(clip_bytes)
            clip_paths.append(str(clip_path))
        job.clip_paths = clip_paths
        logger.info("[%s] all clips downloaded", job_id)

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.COMPOSE, progress=92)
        logger.info("[%s] composing final video", job_id)
        final_video_path = out_dir / "final.mp4"
        compose(clip_paths=clip_paths, audio_path=str(audio_path), out_path=str(final_video_path))
        job_store.set_done(job_id, video_url=f"/jobs/{job_id}/video")
        logger.info("[%s] pipeline complete", job_id)
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.exception("[%s] pipeline failed: %s", job_id, exc)
        job_store.set_error(job_id, str(exc))

input.content:
---
name: hera-api
description: Use this skill when working on CorpuScan code that touches the Hera Motion API — writing prompts for the Hera Agent, editing the HeraClient integration, debugging render submissions, polling, or downloads. Covers endpoints, auth, request/response shapes, status semantics, and the brand/timing constraints CorpuScan layers on top.
---

# Hera Motion API — CorpuScan integration guide

The Hera Motion API generates motion-graphics videos from a **text prompt**.
There is no JSON timeline, no per-element schema, no scene graph — just
prompt + duration + output config in, file URL out.

Hera docs index: <https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt>

---

## Endpoints (base URL `https://api.hera.video/v1`)

| Method | Path                  | Purpose                                    |
| ------ | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| POST   | `/videos`             | Submit a generation job (async).           |
| GET    | `/videos/{video_id}`  | Poll status + collect file URLs on success.|

**Auth:** every request must carry the header `x-api-key: <YOUR_API_KEY>`.

---

## POST `/videos` — request body

```json
{
  "prompt": "A 30-second editorial financial-briefing motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background, 1920x1080 at 30fps. ...",
  "duration_seconds": 30,
  "outputs": [
    { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
  ]
}
```

### Required
- `prompt` (string) — the text description of the motion graphic.
- `outputs` (array, 1–10 items) — at least one export config.

### Optional (CorpuScan currently does not use these)
- `duration_seconds` — int 1–60. Always set this; defaulting is fragile.
- `reference_image_url` (string) or `reference_image_urls` (array, ≤5) — guide the visual style.
- `reference_video_url` (string) — guide motion style.
- `style_id` (string) — apply a preset style.
- `parent_video_id` (string) — iterate on a previous result or template.
- `assets` (array of `{type: "image"|"video"|"audio"|"font"|"csv", url}`) — extra files. Use `POST /files` to upload local files first.

### Output config enums
| Field          | Allowed values                                  |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `format`       | `mp4` `prores` `webm` `gif`                     |
| `aspect_ratio` | `16:9` `9:16` `1:1` `4:5`                       |
| `fps`          | `"24"` `"25"` `"30"` `"60"` (strings, not ints) |
| `resolution`   | `360p` `480p` `720p` `1080p` `4k`               |

> ⚠️ **`fps` is a string, not a number.** Posting `"fps": 30` will be rejected.

### Response
```json
{ "video_id": "vid_abcde12345", "project_url": "https://app.hera.video/..." }
```

---

## GET `/videos/{video_id}` — response

```json
{
  "video_id": "vid_abcde12345",
  "project_url": "https://app.hera.video/...",
  "status": "in-progress" | "success" | "failed",
  "outputs": [
    {
      "status": "in-progress" | "success" | "failed",
      "file_url": "https://cdn.hera.video/...mp4" | null,
      "config": { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" },
      "error": "<message if status=failed>"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Status semantics
- `in-progress` — keep polling.
- `success` — file URLs in `outputs[].file_url` are downloadable.
- `failed` — surface `outputs[i].error`; do NOT retry blindly (likely a prompt issue).

### `404` shape
```json
{ "error": "Video/job not found" }
```

---

## CorpuScan integration

| Concern                | Where it lives                              |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Hera REST client       | `backend/app/integrations/hera.py`          |
| Prompt-writing agent   | `backend/app/agents/hera.py`                |
| Agent system prompt    | `backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`             |
| Pipeline orchestration | `backend/app/pipeline.py` (×4 in parallel)  |

### `HeraClient` contract (in `backend/app/integrations/hera.py`)

- `submit(spec) -> video_id` — POSTs `spec` (the dict the Hera Agent produces) to `/videos`. Validates `prompt` and `outputs` are present.
- `poll(video_id) -> {status, file_url, error}` — GET `/videos/{video_id}`. Normalizes the response: returns the FIRST output's `file_url` (CorpuScan only ever requests one output per render).
- `download(url) -> bytes` — GETs the rendered MP4. **SSRF guard:** rejects any URL that doesn't start with `https://`. Keep this guard.

### `HeraAgent` contract (in `backend/app/agents/hera.py`)

Output is **the request body for `POST /videos`**, ready to hand to `HeraClient.submit`. The agent ALWAYS produces:

```json
{
  "prompt": "<200–500-word natural-language motion-graphics prompt>",
  "duration_seconds": <ceil(max(sentence.end)) clamped to [1, 60]>,
  "outputs": [ { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" } ]
}
```

The pipeline runs four agents in parallel (one per scene) → four `submit()` calls in parallel → poll loop until all four return `success`.

### `_normalize_hera_spec` backstop

Even with a strict prompt, the model occasionally drifts. The agent's
`_normalize_hera_spec` clamps `duration_seconds` to `ceil(max(sentence.end))`
and forces the `outputs` array to the single canonical config. This
avoids a wasted retry round-trip.

---

## Brand & timing constraints (CorpuScan-specific)

The Hera prompt itself is responsible for enforcing CorpuScan's editorial
register. The agent's system prompt (`hera.yaml`) bakes these in:

- **Background** must be solid `#F9FAFB`. No gradients, no images, no video.
- **Color palette** — only these hex values:
  - `#111827` text (headlines, large numbers)
  - `#374151` secondary (sub-labels, axis labels)
  - `#06B6D4` accent (highlights, underlines, the active series)
  - `#F9FAFB` background
- **Typography** — Inter for everything except numerics; JetBrains Mono for raw count-up numbers.
- **Allowed primitives** — kinetic typography, number count-ups, simple bar/line charts (≤6 bars, single series), callout boxes, accent underlines, thin dividers. Nothing else.
- **Beat-by-beat timeline** — every prompt MUST include a per-sentence timeline in `mm:ss.s` format with explicit entry animation, duration, easing, and overlap with the next beat (no gaps to black).
- **Duration math** — `duration_seconds = ceil(max(sentence.end))`, clamped to `[1, 60]`. The model sometimes ignores this; the backstop in the agent corrects it.

---

## Common pitfalls

1. **`fps` as int** — Hera's schema requires it as a string. Validators enforce this.
2. **`aspect_ratio` typos** — must be one of `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:5`. Anything else is silently rejected with a 4xx.
3. **Missing outputs array** — `outputs` is required. Sending only `prompt` will 4xx.
4. **Polling too aggressively** — Hera renders take 30–120s per clip. Poll every 3s, not every 0.5s.
5. **Treating `failed` as transient** — `failed` is usually a prompt issue (banned content, malformed instructions). Do not blindly retry; surface the error to the operator.
6. **Downloading non-https URLs** — the SSRF guard in `HeraClient.download` will reject them. Trust the guard.
7. **Bearer auth** — Hera uses `x-api-key`, NOT `Authorization: Bearer`. The previous CorpuScan implementation got this wrong.

---

## Quick verification recipe

When debugging a render, the smallest end-to-end smoke test is:

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.hera.video/v1/videos \
  -H "x-api-key: $HERA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "prompt": "A 5-second kinetic-typography card on a solid #F9FAFB background that writes the words \"hello world\" in #111827 Inter 700 96px, then fades.",
    "duration_seconds": 5,
    "outputs": [{"format":"mp4","aspect_ratio":"16:9","fps":"30","resolution":"1080p"}]
  }'

# then, with the returned video_id:
curl https://api.hera.video/v1/videos/$VIDEO_ID -H "x-api-key: $HERA_API_KEY"
```

If that round-trip succeeds, the integration plumbing is fine and any
CorpuScan-specific failure is in the agent's prompt (open
`backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`).

# Hera Motion API — quick reference

Canonical AI-agent guide for working with Hera in this repo. The same
content is registered as a Claude Code skill at
[`.claude/skills/hera-api/SKILL.md`](../.claude/skills/hera-api/SKILL.md);
both files should stay in sync.

Hera docs index: <https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt>

---

## Endpoints (`https://api.hera.video/v1`)

| Method | Path                   | Purpose                                   |
| ------ | ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| POST   | `/videos`              | Submit a generation job (async).          |
| GET    | `/videos/{video_id}`   | Poll status + collect file URLs.          |

**Auth:** `x-api-key: <YOUR_API_KEY>` header on every request.

---

## Submit body

```json
{
  "prompt": "<natural-language motion-graphics description>",
  "duration_seconds": 30,
  "outputs": [
    { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
  ]
}
```

- `prompt` and `outputs` are required.
- `duration_seconds` is int 1–60.
- `outputs[].fps` is a STRING (`"30"`, not `30`).

Optional: `reference_image_url(s)`, `reference_video_url`, `style_id`,
`parent_video_id`, `assets` (CorpuScan does not use any of these).

Response: `{ "video_id": "...", "project_url": "..." }`.

---

## Poll response

```json
{
  "video_id": "...",
  "status": "in-progress" | "success" | "failed",
  "outputs": [
    {
      "status": "in-progress" | "success" | "failed",
      "file_url": "https://...mp4" | null,
      "config": {...},
      "error": "<if failed>"
    }
  ]
}
```

CorpuScan only requests ONE output, so use `outputs[0].file_url` once
`status == "success"`.

---

## Where this lives in CorpuScan

- **Client wrapper:** `backend/app/integrations/hera.py` (`HeraClient`)
- **Prompt-writing agent:** `backend/app/agents/hera.py` (`run_hera_agent`)
- **Agent system prompt:** `backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`
- **Pipeline:** `backend/app/pipeline.py` runs 4 agents → 4 submits → poll loop

The Hera Agent's output IS the `POST /videos` request body. The pipeline
hands it directly to `HeraClient.submit`.

---

## Editing the Hera Agent's prompt

The system prompt at `backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml` enforces:

- Brand palette (`#111827` `#374151` `#06B6D4` `#F9FAFB`, no others).
- Typography (Inter; JetBrains Mono for numerics).
- Allowed primitives only (kinetic text, number count-up, bar/line
  charts ≤6 bars, callout box, accent underline, divider).
- Per-sentence beat timeline in `mm:ss.s` format with explicit entry
  animation, duration, easing, and 0.10–0.30s overlap into the next beat.
- `duration_seconds = ceil(max(sentence.end))` clamped to `[1, 60]`.

The agent's `_normalize_hera_spec` backstop clamps `duration_seconds` and
forces the canonical `outputs` config — so even if the model drifts on
those two fields, the request still succeeds.

---

## Pitfalls (Hera-specific gotchas to remember)

1. `fps` is a STRING, not a number. `"30"` ✓, `30` ✗.
2. Auth header is `x-api-key`, NOT `Authorization: Bearer`.
3. Endpoints are `/videos` and `/videos/{video_id}` — NOT `/renders`.
4. Status enum is `in-progress | success | failed` — NOT `done` / `completed` / `succeeded`.
5. File URL lives in `outputs[i].file_url`, NOT a top-level `video_url`.
6. Renders take 30–120s. Poll every 3s.
7. `failed` is usually a prompt problem; surface the error rather than retrying.

name: hera-api
description: Use this skill when working on CorpuScan code that touches the Hera Motion API — writing prompts for the Hera Agent, editing the HeraClient integration, debugging render submissions, polling, or downloads. Covers endpoints, auth, request/response shapes, status semantics, and the brand/timing constraints CorpuScan layers on top.
---

# Hera Motion API — CorpuScan integration guide

The Hera Motion API generates motion-graphics videos from a **text prompt**.
There is no JSON timeline, no per-element schema, no scene graph — just
prompt + duration + output config in, file URL out.

Hera docs index: <https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt>

---

## Endpoints (base URL `https://api.hera.video/v1`)

| Method | Path                  | Purpose                                    |
| ------ | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| POST   | `/videos`             | Submit a generation job (async).           |
| GET    | `/videos/{video_id}`  | Poll status + collect file URLs on success.|

**Auth:** every request must carry the header `x-api-key: <YOUR_API_KEY>`.

---

## POST `/videos` — request body

```json
{
  "prompt": "A 30-second editorial financial-briefing motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background, 1920x1080 at 30fps. ...",
  "duration_seconds": 30,
  "outputs": [
    { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
  ]
}
```

### Required
- `prompt` (string) — the text description of the motion graphic.
- `outputs` (array, 1–10 items) — at least one export config.

### Optional (CorpuScan currently does not use these)
- `duration_seconds` — int 1–60. Always set this; defaulting is fragile.
- `reference_image_url` (string) or `reference_image_urls` (array, ≤5) — guide the visual style.
- `reference_video_url` (string) — guide motion style.
- `style_id` (string) — apply a preset style.
- `parent_video_id` (string) — iterate on a previous result or template.
- `assets` (array of `{type: "image"|"video"|"audio"|"font"|"csv", url}`) — extra files. Use `POST /files` to upload local files first.

### Output config enums
| Field          | Allowed values                                  |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `format`       | `mp4` `prores` `webm` `gif`                     |
| `aspect_ratio` | `16:9` `9:16` `1:1` `4:5`                       |
| `fps`          | `"24"` `"25"` `"30"` `"60"` (strings, not ints) |
| `resolution`   | `360p` `480p` `720p` `1080p` `4k`               |

> ⚠️ **`fps` is a string, not a number.** Posting `"fps": 30` will be rejected.

### Response
```json
{ "video_id": "vid_abcde12345", "project_url": "https://app.hera.video/..." }
```

---

## GET `/videos/{video_id}` — response

```json
{
  "video_id": "vid_abcde12345",
  "project_url": "https://app.hera.video/...",
  "status": "in-progress" | "success" | "failed",
  "outputs": [
    {
      "status": "in-progress" | "success" | "failed",
      "file_url": "https://cdn.hera.video/...mp4" | null,
      "config": { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" },
      "error": "<message if status=failed>"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Status semantics
- `in-progress` — keep polling.
- `success` — file URLs in `outputs[].file_url` are downloadable.
- `failed` — surface `outputs[i].error`; do NOT retry blindly (likely a prompt issue).

### `404` shape
```json
{ "error": "Video/job not found" }
```

---

## CorpuScan integration

| Concern                | Where it lives                              |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Hera REST client       | `backend/app/integrations/hera.py`          |
| Prompt-writing agent   | `backend/app/agents/hera.py`                |
| Agent system prompt    | `backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`             |
| Pipeline orchestration | `backend/app/pipeline.py` (×4 in parallel)  |

### `HeraClient` contract (in `backend/app/integrations/hera.py`)

- `submit(spec) -> video_id` — POSTs `spec` (the dict the Hera Agent produces) to `/videos`. Validates `prompt` and `outputs` are present.
- `poll(video_id) -> {status, file_url, error}` — GET `/videos/{video_id}`. Normalizes the response: returns the FIRST output's `file_url` (CorpuScan only ever requests one output per render).
- `download(url) -> bytes` — GETs the rendered MP4. **SSRF guard:** rejects any URL that doesn't start with `https://`. Keep this guard.

### `HeraAgent` contract (in `backend/app/agents/hera.py`)

Output is **the request body for `POST /videos`**, ready to hand to `HeraClient.submit`. The agent ALWAYS produces:

```json
{
  "prompt": "<200–500-word natural-language motion-graphics prompt>",
  "duration_seconds": <ceil(max(sentence.end)) clamped to [1, 60]>,
  "outputs": [ { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" } ]
}
```

The pipeline runs four agents in parallel (one per scene) → four `submit()` calls in parallel → poll loop until all four return `success`.

### `_normalize_hera_spec` backstop

Even with a strict prompt, the model occasionally drifts. The agent's
`_normalize_hera_spec` clamps `duration_seconds` to `ceil(max(sentence.end))`
and forces the `outputs` array to the single canonical config. This
avoids a wasted retry round-trip.

---

## Brand & timing constraints (CorpuScan-specific)

The Hera prompt itself is responsible for enforcing CorpuScan's editorial
register. The agent's system prompt (`hera.yaml`) bakes these in:

- **Background** must be solid `#F9FAFB`. No gradients, no images, no video.
- **Color palette** — only these hex values:
  - `#111827` text (headlines, large numbers)
  - `#374151` secondary (sub-labels, axis labels)
  - `#06B6D4` accent (highlights, underlines, the active series)
  - `#F9FAFB` background
- **Typography** — Inter for everything except numerics; JetBrains Mono for raw count-up numbers.
- **Allowed primitives** — kinetic typography, number count-ups, simple bar/line charts (≤6 bars, single series), callout boxes, accent underlines, thin dividers. Nothing else.
- **Beat-by-beat timeline** — every prompt MUST include a per-sentence timeline in `mm:ss.s` format with explicit entry animation, duration, easing, and overlap with the next beat (no gaps to black).
- **Duration math** — `duration_seconds = ceil(max(sentence.end))`, clamped to `[1, 60]`. The model sometimes ignores this; the backstop in the agent corrects it.

---

## Common pitfalls

1. **`fps` as int** — Hera's schema requires it as a string. Validators enforce this.
2. **`aspect_ratio` typos** — must be one of `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:5`. Anything else is silently rejected with a 4xx.
3. **Missing outputs array** — `outputs` is required. Sending only `prompt` will 4xx.
4. **Polling too aggressively** — Hera renders take 30–120s per clip. Poll every 3s, not every 0.5s.
5. **Treating `failed` as transient** — `failed` is usually a prompt issue (banned content, malformed instructions). Do not blindly retry; surface the error to the operator.
6. **Downloading non-https URLs** — the SSRF guard in `HeraClient.download` will reject them. Trust the guard.
7. **Bearer auth** — Hera uses `x-api-key`, NOT `Authorization: Bearer`. The previous CorpuScan implementation got this wrong.

---

## Quick verification recipe

When debugging a render, the smallest end-to-end smoke test is:

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.hera.video/v1/videos \
  -H "x-api-key: $HERA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "prompt": "A 5-second kinetic-typography card on a solid #F9FAFB background that writes the words \"hello world\" in #111827 Inter 700 96px, then fades.",
    "duration_seconds": 5,
    "outputs": [{"format":"mp4","aspect_ratio":"16:9","fps":"30","resolution":"1080p"}]
  }'

# then, with the returned video_id:
curl https://api.hera.video/v1/videos/$VIDEO_ID -H "x-api-key: $HERA_API_KEY"
```

If that round-trip succeeds, the integration plumbing is fine and any
CorpuScan-specific failure is in the agent's prompt (open
`backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`).

# Instructions for Claude Code

This file is the entry point for Claude Code working in the CorpuScan repo. The full conventions for any AI coding agent live in [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — read it.

## TL;DR for Claude

- This is **CorpuScan**, a hackathon MVP. Ship over polish.
- **Backend**: FastAPI in `/backend`, Python 3.12, managed with `uv`.
- **Frontend**: React + Vite + Tailwind in `/frontend`, generated by Lovable from `docs/lovable-prompt.md`.
- The product itself contains AI agents (Finance, Scripter, Hera). They are **not** you. Their system prompts live in `backend/app/prompts/{finance,scripter,hera}.yaml`. Do not confuse the product's agents with the AI coding agent (you).
- **No database, no queue, no auth.** Don't add them. If you feel you need one, re-read [docs/PRD.md](docs/PRD.md).
- All API keys are server-side env vars. **Never put a third-party key in the frontend.**

## Where to look

| Question                            | File                                        |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| What is this product?               | [README.md](README.md)                      |
| What are the requirements?          | [docs/PRD.md](docs/PRD.md)                  |
| What's left to build?               | [docs/TASK.md](docs/TASK.md)                |
| Coding conventions, build commands  | [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)                      |
| Frontend scaffold prompt            | [docs/lovable-prompt.md](docs/lovable-prompt.md) |
| Brand voice / colors / typography   | [docs/branding.md](docs/branding.md)        |
| System prompts for in-product AI    | [backend/app/prompts/](backend/app/prompts/) (one YAML per agent) |

## Defaults to follow

- Match the architecture in [README.md](README.md). Mermaid diagram is the source of truth.
- When you complete a step from [TASK.md](TASK.md), tick its checkbox in the same edit.
- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones.
- No comments unless the *why* is non-obvious. No multi-paragraph docstrings.

input.command:
uv run python -c "
import yaml
from app.agents._prompts import load_prompt
from app.agents.hera import validate_hera_spec, _normalize_hera_spec
from app.schemas import SentenceTiming

# YAML loads
for n in ['finance','scripter','hera']:
    p = load_prompt(n); print(n, p.model, 'sys_len=', len(p.system))

# Hera validator: a valid Hera-API request body
ok = {
  'prompt': 'A 5s editorial motion graphic ...',
  'duration_seconds': 5,
  'outputs': [{'format':'mp4','aspect_ratio':'16:9','fps':'30','resolution':'1080p'}],
}
validate_hera_spec(ok); print('valid spec OK')

for bad, label in [
  ({'prompt':'', 'duration_seconds':5, 'outputs':ok['outputs']}, 'empty prompt'),
  ({'prompt':'x', 'duration_seconds':0, 'outputs':ok['outputs']}, 'zero duration'),
  ({'prompt':'x', 'duration_seconds':5, 'outputs':[{'format':'mp4','aspect_ratio':'16:9','fps':30,'resolution':'1080p'}]}, 'fps as int'),
]:
  try:
    validate_hera_spec(bad); print(f'FAIL — {label} accepted')
  except ValueError as e:
    print(f'rejected ({label}):', str(e)[:60])

# Normalize backstop
timings = [SentenceTiming(scene_index=0, sentence='x.', start_seconds=0.0, end_seconds=7.6)]
spec = _normalize_hera_spec({'prompt':'x','duration_seconds':99,'outputs':[]}, timings)
print('normalized:', spec['duration_seconds'], spec['outputs'])
print('OK')
"
