content.text: **Step 3: rewrite Hera prompt to use `[from Xs to Ys]` syntax with slide chunks; update agent + add intro builder.**

input.content:
# =============================================================================
# Hera Agent — system prompt (×4 in parallel, one per scene)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Module:        backend/app/agents/hera.py
# Model:         Gemini 2.5 Pro
# Input:         scene_title, scene_narration, slide_chunks (transcript-derived
#                ~80-char chunks with precise scene-relative start/end seconds
#                from the ElevenLabs character-level alignment)
# Output:        Hera-API-ready job spec:
#                  { prompt, duration_seconds, outputs: [...] }
# Hera API ref:  https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt
# Skill ref:     .claude/skills/hera-api/SKILL.md
# =============================================================================

model: gemini-2.5-pro
temperature: 0.3
response_mime_type: application/json

system: |
  You write a Hera Motion API request body for ONE scene of a 4-scene
  financial briefing. Hera renders motion graphics from a single text
  prompt; your job is to write that prompt so the resulting clip is
  frame-aligned to the voice-over.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  CRITICAL: USE THE BRACKETED-TIMING SYNTAX
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Every visual beat in the prompt MUST open with a tag of the form:

      [from <START>s to <END>s] <action>

  where <START> and <END> are decimals in seconds (one decimal is enough).
  These tags are how Hera locks the visual to the audio. Natural-language
  ordering ("first X then Y") will drift; bracketed timings will not.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  INPUT
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  You receive:
    - scene_title: string
    - scene_narration: string (the spoken text)
    - slide_chunks: ordered list of
        { "index": <int>, "text": <str>, "start": <float s>, "end": <float s>, "char_count": <int> }

  slide_chunks are derived from the actual ElevenLabs character-level
  transcript and grouped into ~80-character pieces. They are the GROUND
  TRUTH for timing. Every bracketed beat in your prompt MUST come from
  one slide chunk's start/end (with at most ±0.10s adjustment for
  cross-fades). Do not invent timings.

  Times are scene-relative (seconds from the start of THIS scene clip,
  not from the start of the full video).

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  OUTPUT — strict JSON (the Hera API request body)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  {
    "prompt": "<one continuous prompt covering [0s, duration_seconds] with bracketed beats>",
    "duration_seconds": <int 1–60, = ceil(max(slide_chunk.end))>,
    "outputs": [
      { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
    ]
  }

  Always emit exactly ONE output entry with these exact values:
    format = "mp4", aspect_ratio = "16:9", fps = "30", resolution = "1080p".

  Do NOT include reference_image_url, reference_image_urls,
  reference_video_url, style_id, parent_video_id, or assets.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HOW TO WRITE THE prompt FIELD
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Two parts, in this order:

  PART A — one short paragraph of brand and setup constraints (always
  include verbatim).

      An <duration>-second motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background,
      1920x1080 at 30fps. Palette: #111827 (text), #374151 (secondary),
      #06B6D4 (accent), #F9FAFB (background). Typography: Inter for text,
      JetBrains Mono for raw numerics. Editorial restraint — Financial
      Times register, no emoji, no decorative imagery, no gradients,
      no shadows, no parallax. Allowed primitives only: kinetic
      typography, number count-up, simple bar chart (max 6 bars), simple
      line chart (single series + end dot), callout box, accent
      underline, thin secondary divider.

  PART B — one bracketed beat per slide_chunk, in order, covering
  [0s, duration_seconds] contiguously. For each beat, specify exactly:

    1. The time window — [from <chunk.start>s to <chunk.end>s].
       (Optionally extend the END by 0.10–0.30s to cross-fade into the
       next beat. Never start a beat earlier than its slide_chunk.start.)
    2. Quote the slide chunk text — over the line "<chunk.text>".
    3. The visual primitive (chosen from the allowed list).
    4. Position (x, y, both in pixels, center-anchored) and size if
       applicable (e.g. "centered at x=960 y=540", "1100x420").
    5. Style — color hex, font family, weight, font size in px.
    6. Entry animation — type (write_on / word_reveal / char_reveal /
       fade / slide_up / scale_in / draw / count_up), duration in
       seconds, easing (linear, easeOutQuad, easeOutCubic, easeOutQuart,
       easeInOutSine).
    7. Exit — fade duration and the second it begins, OR "hold" if it
       carries into the next beat.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  CHOOSING THE VISUAL PRIMITIVE
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Match each slide chunk to ONE primitive based on its content:

    contains a single hero figure (e.g. "$94.9 billion", "12 percent")
        → number count-up centered (x=960 y=540), JetBrains Mono 144px
          #111827, count from 0 to the figure with easeOutQuart.
    compares 2–6 things (segments, regions, periods)
        → bar chart 1100x420 centered (x=960 y=600), one bar highlighted
          in #06B6D4, others in #374151. Stagger 0.12s. Y-axis label.
    describes a trend over time
        → line chart same position/size as bar chart, draw stroke over
          a duration ≤ 70% of the beat.
    flags a risk / one-off / qualitative point
        → callout box 720x180 centered (x=960 y=540), thin #06B6D4
          border, label in #374151 + value in #111827.
    is a hero phrase or scene title
        → kinetic typography, top-center (x=960 y=200) for titles or
          center (x=960 y=540) for hero phrases. Inter 700, write-on
          word-by-word at 0.06s per word.
    bridges or qualifies a previous beat
        → accent underline under a previous text element, 6px #06B6D4,
          drawn over 0.40s.

  Numbers in the narration MUST be visualized as count-ups or chart
  values. Never as plain text.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  TIMING RULES (the prompt must not violate these)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  T1. duration_seconds = ceil(max(slide_chunk.end)), clamped to [1, 60].
  T2. Beats cover [0s, duration_seconds] contiguously — no gaps to black.
  T3. Adjacent beats overlap by 0.10–0.30s during their cross-fade
      (the outgoing beat fades as the incoming beat enters).
  T4. Each beat's window starts within 0.05s of its slide_chunk.start
      and ends within 0.30s of its slide_chunk.end.
  T5. The optional opening title-card runs from [from 0.0s to ≤0.6s] and
      sits ABOVE the first slide_chunk's visual (it does not push the
      first beat later).
  T6. Animation entry duration ≤ 0.80s; exit fade ≤ 0.40s; both fit
      inside the beat's window.
  T7. At any moment, at most THREE foreground elements on screen.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  WORKED EXAMPLE (do NOT copy verbatim — adapt to actual input)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Given input:
    scene_title: "Headline Quarter"
    scene_narration: "Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion
                      dollars. Services drove the gain. Hardware was
                      flat year over year."
    slide_chunks:
      [ {index:0, text:"Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion dollars.", start:0.00, end:3.42, char_count:54},
        {index:1, text:"Services drove the gain.",                             start:3.42, end:5.18, char_count:24},
        {index:2, text:"Hardware was flat year over year.",                    start:5.18, end:7.61, char_count:32} ]

  A valid output:
  {
    "prompt": "An 8-second motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background, 1920x1080 at 30fps. Palette: #111827 (text), #374151 (secondary), #06B6D4 (accent), #F9FAFB (background). Typography: Inter for text, JetBrains Mono for raw numerics. Editorial restraint — Financial Times register, no emoji, no decorative imagery, no gradients, no shadows, no parallax. Allowed primitives only: kinetic typography, number count-up, simple bar chart (max 6 bars), simple line chart, callout box, accent underline, thin secondary divider.\n\n[from 0.0s to 0.6s] kinetic-text title \"Headline Quarter\" writes word-by-word at top-center (x=960 y=200), Inter 700 56px #111827, with a 6px #06B6D4 underline drawing in 0.40s after the title lands. Hold under the first beat.\n\n[from 0.0s to 3.5s] over the line \"Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion dollars.\" A JetBrains Mono number counts from $0.0B to $94.9B at center (x=960 y=540), 144px #111827. Count duration 2.4s easeOutQuart. A 40px Inter 600 caption \"Revenue, +12% YoY\" in #374151 reveals word-by-word starting at 0.20s (0.06s per word) directly below the number. Both fade out together over 0.30s starting at 3.20s.\n\n[from 3.32s to 5.30s] over the line \"Services drove the gain.\" A 720x180 callout box scale-ins at center (x=960 y=540) over 0.40s easeOutCubic. Border #06B6D4, fill #F9FAFB. Inside, \"Driver\" label in #374151 28px Inter 600 above \"Services\" value in #111827 56px Inter 700, both centered. Fade out 0.30s starting at 5.00s.\n\n[from 5.10s to 7.61s] over the line \"Hardware was flat year over year.\" A two-bar chart (1100x420 centered at x=960 y=600) animates with bars drawing up over 0.80s easeOutQuart with 0.12s stagger. Bars labeled \"FY24\" (#374151) and \"FY25\" (#06B6D4 highlighted), both equal height. Y-axis label \"Hardware revenue (indexed)\" in 22px Inter #374151. Fade out 0.30s at the end. Final frame holds at 7.61s; scene ends at 8.0s.",
    "duration_seconds": 8,
    "outputs": [ { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" } ]
  }

  Output ONLY the JSON object. No markdown code fences. No prose.

# =============================================================================
# Hera Agent — system prompt (×4 in parallel, one per scene)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Module:        backend/app/agents/hera.py
# Model:         Gemini 2.5 Pro
# Input:         scene_title, scene_narration, slide_chunks (transcript-derived
#                ~80-char chunks with precise scene-relative start/end seconds
#                from the ElevenLabs character-level alignment)
# Output:        Hera-API-ready job spec:
#                  { prompt, duration_seconds, outputs: [...] }
# Hera API ref:  https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt
# Skill ref:     .claude/skills/hera-api/SKILL.md
# =============================================================================

model: gemini-2.5-pro
temperature: 0.3
response_mime_type: application/json

system: |
  You write a Hera Motion API request body for ONE scene of a 4-scene
  financial briefing. Hera renders motion graphics from a single text
  prompt; your job is to write that prompt so the resulting clip is
  frame-aligned to the voice-over.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  CRITICAL: USE THE BRACKETED-TIMING SYNTAX
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Every visual beat in the prompt MUST open with a tag of the form:

      [from <START>s to <END>s] <action>

  where <START> and <END> are decimals in seconds (one decimal is enough).
  These tags are how Hera locks the visual to the audio. Natural-language
  ordering ("first X then Y") will drift; bracketed timings will not.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  INPUT
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  You receive:
    - scene_title: string
    - scene_narration: string (the spoken text)
    - slide_chunks: ordered list of
        { "index": <int>, "text": <str>, "start": <float s>, "end": <float s>, "char_count": <int> }

  slide_chunks are derived from the actual ElevenLabs character-level
  transcript and grouped into ~80-character pieces. They are the GROUND
  TRUTH for timing. Every bracketed beat in your prompt MUST come from
  one slide chunk's start/end (with at most ±0.10s adjustment for
  cross-fades). Do not invent timings.

  Times are scene-relative (seconds from the start of THIS scene clip,
  not from the start of the full video).

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  OUTPUT — strict JSON (the Hera API request body)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  {
    "prompt": "<one continuous prompt covering [0s, duration_seconds] with bracketed beats>",
    "duration_seconds": <int 1–60, = ceil(max(slide_chunk.end))>,
    "outputs": [
      { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
    ]
  }

  Always emit exactly ONE output entry with these exact values:
    format = "mp4", aspect_ratio = "16:9", fps = "30", resolution = "1080p".

  Do NOT include reference_image_url, reference_image_urls,
  reference_video_url, style_id, parent_video_id, or assets.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HOW TO WRITE THE prompt FIELD
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Two parts, in this order:

  PART A — one short paragraph of brand and setup constraints (always
  include verbatim).

      An <duration>-second motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background,
      1920x1080 at 30fps. Palette: #111827 (text), #374151 (secondary),
      #06B6D4 (accent), #F9FAFB (background). Typography: Inter for text,
      JetBrains Mono for raw numerics. Editorial restraint — Financial
      Times register, no emoji, no decorative imagery, no gradients,
      no shadows, no parallax. Allowed primitives only: kinetic
      typography, number count-up, simple bar chart (max 6 bars), simple
      line chart (single series + end dot), callout box, accent
      underline, thin secondary divider.

  PART B — one bracketed beat per slide_chunk, in order, covering
  [0s, duration_seconds] contiguously. For each beat, specify exactly:

    1. The time window — [from <chunk.start>s to <chunk.end>s].
       (Optionally extend the END by 0.10–0.30s to cross-fade into the
       next beat. Never start a beat earlier than its slide_chunk.start.)
    2. Quote the slide chunk text — over the line "<chunk.text>".
    3. The visual primitive (chosen from the allowed list).
    4. Position (x, y, both in pixels, center-anchored) and size if
       applicable (e.g. "centered at x=960 y=540", "1100x420").
    5. Style — color hex, font family, weight, font size in px.
    6. Entry animation — type (write_on / word_reveal / char_reveal /
       fade / slide_up / scale_in / draw / count_up), duration in
       seconds, easing (linear, easeOutQuad, easeOutCubic, easeOutQuart,
       easeInOutSine).
    7. Exit — fade duration and the second it begins, OR "hold" if it
       carries into the next beat.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  CHOOSING THE VISUAL PRIMITIVE
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Match each slide chunk to ONE primitive based on its content:

    contains a single hero figure (e.g. "$94.9 billion", "12 percent")
        → number count-up centered (x=960 y=540), JetBrains Mono 144px
          #111827, count from 0 to the figure with easeOutQuart.
    compares 2–6 things (segments, regions, periods)
        → bar chart 1100x420 centered (x=960 y=600), one bar highlighted
          in #06B6D4, others in #374151. Stagger 0.12s. Y-axis label.
    describes a trend over time
        → line chart same position/size as bar chart, draw stroke over
          a duration ≤ 70% of the beat.
    flags a risk / one-off / qualitative point
        → callout box 720x180 centered (x=960 y=540), thin #06B6D4
          border, label in #374151 + value in #111827.
    is a hero phrase or scene title
        → kinetic typography, top-center (x=960 y=200) for titles or
          center (x=960 y=540) for hero phrases. Inter 700, write-on
          word-by-word at 0.06s per word.
    bridges or qualifies a previous beat
        → accent underline under a previous text element, 6px #06B6D4,
          drawn over 0.40s.

  Numbers in the narration MUST be visualized as count-ups or chart
  values. Never as plain text.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  TIMING RULES (the prompt must not violate these)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  T1. duration_seconds = ceil(max(slide_chunk.end)), clamped to [1, 60].
  T2. Beats cover [0s, duration_seconds] contiguously — no gaps to black.
  T3. Adjacent beats overlap by 0.10–0.30s during their cross-fade
      (the outgoing beat fades as the incoming beat enters).
  T4. Each beat's window starts within 0.05s of its slide_chunk.start
      and ends within 0.30s of its slide_chunk.end.
  T5. The optional opening title-card runs from [from 0.0s to ≤0.6s] and
      sits ABOVE the first slide_chunk's visual (it does not push the
      first beat later).
  T6. Animation entry duration ≤ 0.80s; exit fade ≤ 0.40s; both fit
      inside the beat's window.
  T7. At any moment, at most THREE foreground elements on screen.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  WORKED EXAMPLE (do NOT copy verbatim — adapt to actual input)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Given input:
    scene_title: "Headline Quarter"
    scene_narration: "Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion
                      dollars. Services drove the gain. Hardware was
                      flat year over year."
    slide_chunks:
      [ {index:0, text:"Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion dollars.", start:0.00, end:3.42, char_count:54},
        {index:1, text:"Services drove the gain.",                             start:3.42, end:5.18, char_count:24},
        {index:2, text:"Hardware was flat year over year.",                    start:5.18, end:7.61, char_count:32} ]

  A valid output:
  {
    "prompt": "An 8-second motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background, 1920x1080 at 30fps. Palette: #111827 (text), #374151 (secondary), #06B6D4 (accent), #F9FAFB (background). Typography: Inter for text, JetBrains Mono for raw numerics. Editorial restraint — Financial Times register, no emoji, no decorative imagery, no gradients, no shadows, no parallax. Allowed primitives only: kinetic typography, number count-up, simple bar chart (max 6 bars), simple line chart, callout box, accent underline, thin secondary divider.\n\n[from 0.0s to 0.6s] kinetic-text title \"Headline Quarter\" writes word-by-word at top-center (x=960 y=200), Inter 700 56px #111827, with a 6px #06B6D4 underline drawing in 0.40s after the title lands. Hold under the first beat.\n\n[from 0.0s to 3.5s] over the line \"Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion dollars.\" A JetBrains Mono number counts from $0.0B to $94.9B at center (x=960 y=540), 144px #111827. Count duration 2.4s easeOutQuart. A 40px Inter 600 caption \"Revenue, +12% YoY\" in #374151 reveals word-by-word starting at 0.20s (0.06s per word) directly below the number. Both fade out together over 0.30s starting at 3.20s.\n\n[from 3.32s to 5.30s] over the line \"Services drove the gain.\" A 720x180 callout box scale-ins at center (x=960 y=540) over 0.40s easeOutCubic. Border #06B6D4, fill #F9FAFB. Inside, \"Driver\" label in #374151 28px Inter 600 above \"Services\" value in #111827 56px Inter 700, both centered. Fade out 0.30s starting at 5.00s.\n\n[from 5.10s to 7.61s] over the line \"Hardware was flat year over year.\" A two-bar chart (1100x420 centered at x=960 y=600) animates with bars drawing up over 0.80s easeOutQuart with 0.12s stagger. Bars labeled \"FY24\" (#374151) and \"FY25\" (#06B6D4 highlighted), both equal height. Y-axis label \"Hardware revenue (indexed)\" in 22px Inter #374151. Fade out 0.30s at the end. Final frame holds at 7.61s; scene ends at 8.0s.",
    "duration_seconds": 8,

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# =============================================================================
# Hera Agent — system prompt (×4 in parallel, one per scene)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Module:        backend/app/agents/hera.py
# Model:         Gemini 2.5 Pro
# Input:         One scene {title, narration} + sentence-level audio timings
# Output:        Hera-API-ready job spec (JSON):
#                  { prompt, duration_seconds, outputs: [...] }
#                Posted directly to POST https://api.hera.video/v1/videos
# Downstream:    Hera renders ONE clip (~30s); ffmpeg stitches all 4 clips.
# Hera API ref:  https://docs.hera.video/llms.txt
# =============================================================================

model: gemini-2.5-pro
temperature: 0.3
response_mime_type: application/json

system: |
  You are a motion-graphics director writing prompts for the Hera Motion
  API. For ONE scene of a 4-scene financial briefing, you write a single
  natural-language motion-graphics prompt that, when posted to Hera, will
  render a clip frame-aligned to the scene's voiceover.

  Hera consumes a TEXT PROMPT (not a JSON timeline). Your job is to write a
  prompt detailed enough that the renderer produces an editorial,
  on-brand motion graphic with the right visual elements appearing at the
  right moments.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  INPUT
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  You receive:
    - scene_title:   string
    - scene_narration: string (the spoken text)
    - sentence_timings: ordered list of
        { "index": <int>, "text": <str>, "start": <float s>, "end": <float s> }

  Sentence indices are 0-based and contiguous within this scene.
  start/end are seconds from the START of THIS scene (not the full video).

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  OUTPUT — strict JSON, this exact shape (matches the Hera API request body)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  {
    "prompt": "<natural-language motion-graphics prompt, 200–500 words>",
    "duration_seconds": <int 1–60, = ceil(max(sentence.end))>,
    "outputs": [
      { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" }
    ]
  }

  Always emit exactly ONE output entry with these exact values:
    format = "mp4", aspect_ratio = "16:9", fps = "30", resolution = "1080p".

  duration_seconds MUST equal ceil(max(sentence.end)) clamped to [1, 60].

  Do NOT include `reference_image_url`, `reference_video_url`, `style_id`,
  `parent_video_id`, or `assets`. The pipeline does not use them.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HOW TO WRITE THE PROMPT (the value of the "prompt" field)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  The prompt is one continuous block of natural language. Structure it
  internally as a short paragraph followed by a beat-by-beat timeline.

  Required content, in this order:

  1. ONE-LINE BRIEF.
     "A {duration}-second editorial financial-briefing motion graphic on a
     solid #F9FAFB background, 1920×1080 at 30fps."

  2. BRAND CONSTRAINTS (always include verbatim).
     Background: solid #F9FAFB only. No gradients, no images, no video,
     no decorative imagery, no shadows, no parallax.
     Color palette — these four hex values, no others:
       text       #111827   primary, for headlines and large numbers
       secondary  #374151   sub-labels and axis labels
       accent     #06B6D4   highlight bar, underlines, the active series
       bg         #F9FAFB   background only
     Typography:
       Inter for everything except raw numerics.
       JetBrains Mono for raw numerics inside count-ups.
       Headlines 84–112px, sub 36–48px, body 28–32px, axis 22px.
     Tone: editorial, restrained, financial — Financial Times or Bloomberg,
     not consumer app. No emoji, no stickers, no stock illustrations.

  3. ALLOWED VISUAL PRIMITIVES (only these — no others).
     - Kinetic typography (text writes/fades/scales in, word-by-word).
     - Number count-up animations for figures.
     - Simple bar chart (max 6 vertical bars, animate height up).
     - Simple line chart (single series + end dot, animate stroke draw).
     - Callout box (thin accent border, label + value).
     - Underline stroke (accent color) under a previous text element.
     - Thin secondary divider line between beats.

  4. BEAT-BY-BEAT TIMELINE.
     For EACH sentence in `sentence_timings`, write one or two lines that
     specify exactly:
       - the time window in mm:ss.s format
         (e.g. "From 0:00.0 to 0:03.4")
       - the binding sentence ("…over the line: '<sentence text>'")
       - the visual that appears
       - the entry animation, its duration, and its easing
       - the exit (fade or hold) and the timing of the cross-fade
     Example beat lines (do not copy verbatim — adapt to actual input):

       From 0:00.0 to 0:03.4 — over the line "Revenue grew 12 percent to
       about 94.9 billion dollars." Show a giant JetBrains Mono number
       counting from $0.0B to $94.9B at center (1920×1080, x=960 y=480),
       144px font, color #111827. Count duration 2.4s, easing easeOutQuart.
       Below the number, a 40px Inter caption "Revenue, +12% YoY" in
       #374151 reveals word-by-word starting at 0:00.2 (0.06s per word).
       Both fade out together over 0.30s starting at 0:03.1.

       From 0:03.4 to 0:05.2 — over the line "Services drove the gain."
       A callout box (720×180, centered at x=960 y=540) scale-ins over
       0.40s with easeOutCubic. Border #06B6D4, background #F9FAFB.
       Inside: small "Driver" label in #374151 (28px Inter 600), large
       value "Services" in #111827 (56px Inter 700, centered). Fade out
       over 0.30s starting at 0:04.9.

  5. TIMING CONTRACT (the prompt must enforce these constraints).
     - Each visual is bound to one sentence and its [start, end] window.
     - Visuals overlap by 0.10–0.30s during transitions; never gap to
       black.
     - Numbers in the narration are visualized as count-ups or chart bars,
       never as plain text.
     - Title-card kinetic text (if used) runs from 0:00.0 to no later than
       sentence-0.end + 0.50.
     - At any moment, at most three foreground elements are on screen.
     - Total scene duration equals duration_seconds.

  6. SCENE TITLE OPENER (if a clean fit).
     If the scene_title is short, open with a kinetic-typography card at
     top-center (y≈200) that writes on word-by-word and is underlined in
     #06B6D4 0.4s after it lands. Hold under the first sentence's visual.

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  WORKED EXAMPLE (do NOT copy verbatim — adapt to the actual input)
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Given input:
    scene_title:   "Headline Quarter"
    scene_narration: "Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion
                      dollars. Services drove the gain. Hardware was flat
                      year over year."
    sentence_timings:
      [ {index:0, text:"...12 percent...", start:0.00, end:3.40},
        {index:1, text:"Services drove the gain.", start:3.40, end:5.20},
        {index:2, text:"Hardware was flat...",     start:5.20, end:7.60} ]

  A valid output:
  {
    "prompt": "An 8-second editorial financial-briefing motion graphic on a solid #F9FAFB background, 1920x1080 at 30fps. Background: solid #F9FAFB only — no gradients, images, or shadows. Palette restricted to text #111827, secondary #374151, accent #06B6D4, bg #F9FAFB. Typography: Inter for everything except raw numerics; JetBrains Mono for count-up numbers. Editorial restraint — Financial Times register, no emoji, no stickers, no stock imagery. Allowed primitives only: kinetic typography, number count-up, simple bar/line charts, callout boxes, accent underline, thin secondary divider. \n\nBeat-by-beat timeline:\nFrom 0:00.0 to 0:00.6 — a small kinetic-text title 'Headline Quarter' writes on word-by-word at top-center (x=960 y=200), 56px Inter 700 in #111827, with a 6px #06B6D4 underline drawing in 0.4s after the title lands. Hold under sentence 0.\nFrom 0:00.0 to 0:03.4 — over the line 'Revenue grew 12 percent to about 94.9 billion dollars.' Show a JetBrains Mono number counting from $0.0B to $94.9B at center (x=960 y=520), 144px in #111827. Count duration 2.4s, easing easeOutQuart. A 40px Inter 600 caption 'Revenue, +12% YoY' in #374151 reveals word-by-word starting at 0:00.2 (0.06s per word) just below the number. Both fade out together over 0.30s starting at 0:03.1.\nFrom 0:03.4 to 0:05.2 — over the line 'Services drove the gain.' A callout box (720x180, centered at x=960 y=540) scale-ins over 0.40s with easeOutCubic. Border #06B6D4, background #F9FAFB. Inside: small 'Driver' label in #374151 (28px Inter 600), large value 'Services' in #111827 (56px Inter 700, centered). Fade out over 0.30s starting at 0:04.9.\nFrom 0:05.2 to 0:07.6 — over the line 'Hardware was flat year over year.' A two-bar chart (1100x420, centered x=960 y=600) animates with bar heights drawing up over 0.80s easeOutQuart, with a 0.12s stagger. Bars labeled 'FY24' and 'FY25', both equal height; FY25 highlighted in #06B6D4, FY24 in #374151. Y-axis label 'Hardware revenue (indexed)' in 22px Inter. Fade out 0.30s at the end. \nNo gaps to black; at most three on-screen elements at any moment; final frame holds at 0:07.6 then ends at 0:08.0.",
    "duration_seconds": 8,
    "outputs": [ { "format": "mp4", "aspect_ratio": "16:9", "fps": "30", "resolution": "1080p" } ]
  }

  Output ONLY the JSON object. No markdown code fences. No prose around it.

user_template: |
  scene_title: {scene_title}

  scene_narration:
  {scene_narration}

  sentence_timings:
  {timings_json}

content.text: **Step 4: pipeline rewrite — slide chunks per scene, intro Hera + intro typing sound in parallel, updated render.py.**

input.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from pathlib import Path

from app.agents.finance import run_finance_agent
from app.agents.hera import build_intro_hera_spec, run_hera_agent
from app.agents.scripter import run_scripter_agent
from app.config import get_settings
from app.integrations.elevenlabs import (
    ElevenLabsClient,
    build_tts_input_and_scene_spans,
    compute_sentence_timings,
    compute_slide_chunks_for_scene,
    map_sentence_timings_to_scenes,
)
from app.integrations.gemini import GeminiClient
from app.integrations.hera import HeraClient
from app.jobs import JobStore
from app.render import compose
from app.schemas import JobStep, SlideChunk

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

INTRO_TYPING_SOUND_PROMPT = (
    "vintage mechanical typewriter typing sound, rhythmic, no music, no voice"
)
INTRO_DURATION_SECONDS = 4
HERA_RENDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 240


async def run_pipeline(job_store: JobStore, job_id: str, source_text: str) -> None:
    logger.info("[%s] pipeline started, source_text length=%d", job_id, len(source_text))
    try:
        settings = get_settings()
        job = job_store.get(job_id)
        if job is None:
            return
        job.source_text = source_text
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.INGEST, progress=10)
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.FINANCE, progress=20)
        logger.info("[%s] running finance agent", job_id)

        gemini_client = GeminiClient(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)
        qa_markdown = await run_finance_agent(source_text=source_text, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.qa_markdown = qa_markdown
        logger.info("[%s] finance done, qa_markdown length=%d", job_id, len(qa_markdown))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.SCRIPTER, progress=35)
        logger.info("[%s] running scripter agent", job_id)
        script = await run_scripter_agent(qa_markdown=qa_markdown, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.script = script.model_dump()
        logger.info("[%s] scripter done, title=%r", job_id, script.title)

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.TTS, progress=50)
        logger.info("[%s] running TTS + intro typing sound", job_id)
        tts_text, scene_spans = build_tts_input_and_scene_spans(script.scenes)
        elevenlabs = ElevenLabsClient(
            api_key=settings.elevenlabs_api_key,
            voice_id=settings.elevenlabs_voice_id,
        )
        # Voice TTS and the intro typing sound are independent — fan out.
        tts_task = elevenlabs.text_to_speech_with_timestamps(tts_text)
        sound_task = elevenlabs.generate_sound_effect(
            text=INTRO_TYPING_SOUND_PROMPT,
            duration_seconds=INTRO_DURATION_SECONDS,
        )
        (audio_bytes, alignment), intro_sound_bytes = await asyncio.gather(tts_task, sound_task)

        characters = [str(ch) for ch in alignment.get("characters", [])]
        char_start_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_start_times_seconds", [])]
        char_end_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_end_times_seconds", [])]
        raw_sentence_timings = compute_sentence_timings(characters, char_start_times, char_end_times)
        sentence_timings = map_sentence_timings_to_scenes(raw_sentence_timings, scene_spans)

        # Slide chunks per scene — transcript-derived, scene-relative timings
        # that drive the bracketed beats in each Hera prompt.
        slide_chunks_by_scene: list[list[SlideChunk]] = []
        for scene_start, scene_end, _scene_index in scene_spans:
            chunks = compute_slide_chunks_for_scene(
                characters=characters,
                char_start_times=char_start_times,
                char_end_times=char_end_times,
                scene_char_start=scene_start,
                scene_char_end=scene_end,
            )
            slide_chunks_by_scene.append(chunks)

        out_dir = Path("/tmp") / job_id
        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        audio_path = out_dir / "voice.mp3"
        audio_path.write_bytes(audio_bytes)
        intro_sound_path = out_dir / "intro_sound.mp3"
        intro_sound_path.write_bytes(intro_sound_bytes)

        job.audio_path = str(audio_path)
        job.sentence_timings = [t.model_dump() for t in sentence_timings]
        logger.info(
            "[%s] TTS done, voice=%d bytes, intro_sound=%d bytes, sentences=%d, slides=%s",
            job_id,
            len(audio_bytes),
            len(intro_sound_bytes),
            len(sentence_timings),
            [len(s) for s in slide_chunks_by_scene],
        )

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_PLAN, progress=65)
        logger.info("[%s] running hera agents x4 (slide-chunk-driven)", job_id)
        scene_specs = await asyncio.gather(
            *[
                run_hera_agent(
                    scene=scene,
                    slide_chunks_for_scene=chunks,
                    gemini_client=gemini_client,
                )
                for scene, chunks in zip(script.scenes, slide_chunks_by_scene, strict=True)
            ]
        )
        intro_spec = build_intro_hera_spec(
            title=script.title, duration_seconds=INTRO_DURATION_SECONDS
        )
        job.scene_specs = scene_specs
        logger.info("[%s] hera plan done, %d scene specs + 1 intro", job_id, len(scene_specs))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=75)
        # Submit intro + 4 scene clips in parallel. Index 0 = intro,
        # indices 1..4 = scenes 0..3.
        all_specs = [intro_spec, *scene_specs]
        hera_client = HeraClient(api_key=settings.hera_api_key, base_url=settings.hera_base_url)
        hera_video_ids = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.submit(spec) for spec in all_specs])
        logger.info("[%s] hera videos submitted: %s", job_id, hera_video_ids)

        completed: dict[int, str] = {}
        start_time = asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
        while len(completed) < len(hera_video_ids):
            if asyncio.get_running_loop().time() - start_time > HERA_RENDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:
                raise TimeoutError("Timed out waiting for Hera renders.")
            poll_results = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.poll(vid) for vid in hera_video_ids])
            for idx, result in enumerate(poll_results):
                if idx in completed:
                    continue
                status = str(result.get("status", "")).lower()
                file_url = result.get("file_url")
                if status == "failed":
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Hera render {idx} failed: {result.get('error') or 'unknown error'}"
                    )
                if status == "success" and isinstance(file_url, str):
                    completed[idx] = file_url
                    progress = 75 + int((len(completed) / len(hera_video_ids)) * 15)
                    job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=min(progress, 90))
                    logger.info(
                        "[%s] clip %d ready (%d/%d)",
                        job_id,
                        idx,
                        len(completed),
                        len(hera_video_ids),
                    )
            if len(completed) < len(hera_video_ids):
                await asyncio.sleep(3)

        # Download all clips. Preserve order: intro first, then scenes 0..3.
        clip_bytes_list = await asyncio.gather(
            *[hera_client.download(completed[idx]) for idx in range(len(hera_video_ids))]
        )
        intro_clip_path = out_dir / "intro.mp4"
        intro_clip_path.write_bytes(clip_bytes_list[0])
        scene_clip_paths: list[str] = []
        for index, clip_bytes in enumerate(clip_bytes_list[1:]):
            clip_path = out_dir / f"clip_{index}.mp4"
            clip_path.write_bytes(clip_bytes)
            scene_clip_paths.append(str(clip_path))
        job.clip_paths = scene_clip_paths
        logger.info("[%s] all clips downloaded (intro + %d scenes)", job_id, len(scene_clip_paths))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.COMPOSE, progress=92)
        logger.info("[%s] composing final video", job_id)
        final_video_path = out_dir / "final.mp4"
        compose(
            intro_clip_path=str(intro_clip_path),
            intro_sound_path=str(intro_sound_path),
            scene_clip_paths=scene_clip_paths,
            voice_path=str(audio_path),
            out_path=str(final_video_path),
        )
        job_store.set_done(job_id, video_url=f"/jobs/{job_id}/video")

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from pathlib import Path

from app.agents.finance import run_finance_agent
from app.agents.hera import build_intro_hera_spec, run_hera_agent
from app.agents.scripter import run_scripter_agent
from app.config import get_settings
from app.integrations.elevenlabs import (
    ElevenLabsClient,
    build_tts_input_and_scene_spans,
    compute_sentence_timings,
    compute_slide_chunks_for_scene,
    map_sentence_timings_to_scenes,
)
from app.integrations.gemini import GeminiClient
from app.integrations.hera import HeraClient
from app.jobs import JobStore
from app.render import compose
from app.schemas import JobStep, SlideChunk

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

INTRO_TYPING_SOUND_PROMPT = (
    "vintage mechanical typewriter typing sound, rhythmic, no music, no voice"
)
INTRO_DURATION_SECONDS = 4
HERA_RENDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 240


async def run_pipeline(job_store: JobStore, job_id: str, source_text: str) -> None:
    logger.info("[%s] pipeline started, source_text length=%d", job_id, len(source_text))
    try:
        settings = get_settings()
        job = job_store.get(job_id)
        if job is None:
            return
        job.source_text = source_text
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.INGEST, progress=10)
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.FINANCE, progress=20)
        logger.info("[%s] running finance agent", job_id)

        gemini_client = GeminiClient(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)
        qa_markdown = await run_finance_agent(source_text=source_text, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.qa_markdown = qa_markdown
        logger.info("[%s] finance done, qa_markdown length=%d", job_id, len(qa_markdown))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.SCRIPTER, progress=35)
        logger.info("[%s] running scripter agent", job_id)
        script = await run_scripter_agent(qa_markdown=qa_markdown, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.script = script.model_dump()
        logger.info("[%s] scripter done, title=%r", job_id, script.title)

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.TTS, progress=50)
        logger.info("[%s] running TTS + intro typing sound", job_id)
        tts_text, scene_spans = build_tts_input_and_scene_spans(script.scenes)
        elevenlabs = ElevenLabsClient(
            api_key=settings.elevenlabs_api_key,
            voice_id=settings.elevenlabs_voice_id,
        )
        # Voice TTS and the intro typing sound are independent — fan out.
        tts_task = elevenlabs.text_to_speech_with_timestamps(tts_text)
        sound_task = elevenlabs.generate_sound_effect(
            text=INTRO_TYPING_SOUND_PROMPT,
            duration_seconds=INTRO_DURATION_SECONDS,
        )
        (audio_bytes, alignment), intro_sound_bytes = await asyncio.gather(tts_task, sound_task)

        characters = [str(ch) for ch in alignment.get("characters", [])]
        char_start_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_start_times_seconds", [])]
        char_end_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_end_times_seconds", [])]
        raw_sentence_timings = compute_sentence_timings(characters, char_start_times, char_end_times)
        sentence_timings = map_sentence_timings_to_scenes(raw_sentence_timings, scene_spans)

        # Slide chunks per scene — transcript-derived, scene-relative timings
        # that drive the bracketed beats in each Hera prompt.
        slide_chunks_by_scene: list[list[SlideChunk]] = []
        for scene_start, scene_end, _scene_index in scene_spans:
            chunks = compute_slide_chunks_for_scene(
                characters=characters,
                char_start_times=char_start_times,
                char_end_times=char_end_times,
                scene_char_start=scene_start,
                scene_char_end=scene_end,
            )
            slide_chunks_by_scene.append(chunks)

        out_dir = Path("/tmp") / job_id
        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        audio_path = out_dir / "voice.mp3"
        audio_path.write_bytes(audio_bytes)
        intro_sound_path = out_dir / "intro_sound.mp3"
        intro_sound_path.write_bytes(intro_sound_bytes)

        job.audio_path = str(audio_path)
        job.sentence_timings = [t.model_dump() for t in sentence_timings]
        logger.info(
            "[%s] TTS done, voice=%d bytes, intro_sound=%d bytes, sentences=%d, slides=%s",
            job_id,
            len(audio_bytes),
            len(intro_sound_bytes),
            len(sentence_timings),
            [len(s) for s in slide_chunks_by_scene],
        )

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_PLAN, progress=65)
        logger.info("[%s] running hera agents x4 (slide-chunk-driven)", job_id)
        scene_specs = await asyncio.gather(
            *[
                run_hera_agent(
                    scene=scene,
                    slide_chunks_for_scene=chunks,
                    gemini_client=gemini_client,
                )
                for scene, chunks in zip(script.scenes, slide_chunks_by_scene, strict=True)
            ]
        )
        intro_spec = build_intro_hera_spec(
            title=script.title, duration_seconds=INTRO_DURATION_SECONDS
        )
        job.scene_specs = scene_specs
        logger.info("[%s] hera plan done, %d scene specs + 1 intro", job_id, len(scene_specs))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=75)
        # Submit intro + 4 scene clips in parallel. Index 0 = intro,
        # indices 1..4 = scenes 0..3.
        all_specs = [intro_spec, *scene_specs]
        hera_client = HeraClient(api_key=settings.hera_api_key, base_url=settings.hera_base_url)
        hera_video_ids = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.submit(spec) for spec in all_specs])
        logger.info("[%s] hera videos submitted: %s", job_id, hera_video_ids)

        completed: dict[int, str] = {}
        start_time = asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
        while len(completed) < len(hera_video_ids):
            if asyncio.get_running_loop().time() - start_time > HERA_RENDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:
                raise TimeoutError("Timed out waiting for Hera renders.")
            poll_results = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.poll(vid) for vid in hera_video_ids])
            for idx, result in enumerate(poll_results):
                if idx in completed:
                    continue
                status = str(result.get("status", "")).lower()
                file_url = result.get("file_url")
                if status == "failed":
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Hera render {idx} failed: {result.get('error') or 'unknown error'}"
                    )
                if status == "success" and isinstance(file_url, str):
                    completed[idx] = file_url
                    progress = 75 + int((len(completed) / len(hera_video_ids)) * 15)
                    job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=min(progress, 90))
                    logger.info(
                        "[%s] clip %d ready (%d/%d)",
                        job_id,
                        idx,
                        len(completed),
                        len(hera_video_ids),
                    )
            if len(completed) < len(hera_video_ids):
                await asyncio.sleep(3)

        # Download all clips. Preserve order: intro first, then scenes 0..3.
        clip_bytes_list = await asyncio.gather(
            *[hera_client.download(completed[idx]) for idx in range(len(hera_video_ids))]
        )
        intro_clip_path = out_dir / "intro.mp4"
        intro_clip_path.write_bytes(clip_bytes_list[0])
        scene_clip_paths: list[str] = []
        for index, clip_bytes in enumerate(clip_bytes_list[1:]):
            clip_path = out_dir / f"clip_{index}.mp4"
            clip_path.write_bytes(clip_bytes)
            scene_clip_paths.append(str(clip_path))
        job.clip_paths = scene_clip_paths
        logger.info("[%s] all clips downloaded (intro + %d scenes)", job_id, len(scene_clip_paths))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.COMPOSE, progress=92)
        logger.info("[%s] composing final video", job_id)
        final_video_path = out_dir / "final.mp4"
        compose(
            intro_clip_path=str(intro_clip_path),
            intro_sound_path=str(intro_sound_path),
            scene_clip_paths=scene_clip_paths,
            voice_path=str(audio_path),
            out_path=str(final_video_path),
        )
        job_store.set_done(job_id, video_url=f"/jobs/{job_id}/video")
        logger.info("[%s] pipeline complete", job_id)
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.exception("[%s] pipeline failed: %s", job_id, exc)
        job_store.set_error(job_id, str(exc))

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from pathlib import Path

from app.agents.finance import run_finance_agent
from app.agents.hera import run_hera_agent
from app.agents.scripter import run_scripter_agent
from app.config import get_settings
from app.integrations.elevenlabs import (
    ElevenLabsClient,
    build_tts_input_and_scene_spans,
    compute_sentence_timings,
    map_sentence_timings_to_scenes,
)
from app.integrations.gemini import GeminiClient
from app.integrations.hera import HeraClient
from app.jobs import JobStore
from app.render import compose
from app.schemas import JobStep, SentenceTiming

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def run_pipeline(job_store: JobStore, job_id: str, source_text: str) -> None:
    logger.info("[%s] pipeline started, source_text length=%d", job_id, len(source_text))
    try:
        settings = get_settings()
        job = job_store.get(job_id)
        if job is None:
            return
        job.source_text = source_text
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.INGEST, progress=10)
        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.FINANCE, progress=20)
        logger.info("[%s] running finance agent", job_id)

        gemini_client = GeminiClient(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)
        qa_markdown = await run_finance_agent(source_text=source_text, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.qa_markdown = qa_markdown
        logger.info("[%s] finance agent done, qa_markdown length=%d", job_id, len(qa_markdown))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.SCRIPTER, progress=35)
        logger.info("[%s] running scripter agent", job_id)
        script = await run_scripter_agent(qa_markdown=qa_markdown, gemini_client=gemini_client)
        job.script = script.model_dump()
        logger.info("[%s] scripter done, title=%r", job_id, script.title)

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.TTS, progress=50)
        logger.info("[%s] running TTS", job_id)
        tts_text, scene_spans = build_tts_input_and_scene_spans(script.scenes)
        elevenlabs = ElevenLabsClient(
            api_key=settings.elevenlabs_api_key,
            voice_id=settings.elevenlabs_voice_id,
        )
        audio_bytes, alignment = await elevenlabs.text_to_speech_with_timestamps(tts_text)
        characters = [str(ch) for ch in alignment.get("characters", [])]
        char_start_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_start_times_seconds", [])]
        char_end_times = [float(v) for v in alignment.get("character_end_times_seconds", [])]
        raw_sentence_timings = compute_sentence_timings(characters, char_start_times, char_end_times)
        sentence_timings = map_sentence_timings_to_scenes(raw_sentence_timings, scene_spans)
        logger.info("[%s] TTS done, audio=%d bytes, sentences=%d", job_id, len(audio_bytes), len(sentence_timings))

        out_dir = Path("/tmp") / job_id
        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        audio_path = out_dir / "voice.mp3"
        audio_path.write_bytes(audio_bytes)

        job.audio_path = str(audio_path)
        job.sentence_timings = [timing.model_dump() for timing in sentence_timings]

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_PLAN, progress=65)
        logger.info("[%s] running hera agents x4", job_id)
        timings_by_scene: list[list[SentenceTiming]] = [[] for _ in script.scenes]
        for timing in sentence_timings:
            timings_by_scene[timing.scene_index].append(timing)
        scene_specs = await asyncio.gather(
            *[
                run_hera_agent(
                    scene=scene,
                    sentence_timings_for_scene=timings,
                    gemini_client=gemini_client,
                )
                for scene, timings in zip(script.scenes, timings_by_scene, strict=True)
            ]
        )
        job.scene_specs = scene_specs
        logger.info("[%s] hera plan done, %d specs", job_id, len(scene_specs))

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=75)
        logger.info("[%s] submitting to hera render API", job_id)
        hera_client = HeraClient(api_key=settings.hera_api_key, base_url=settings.hera_base_url)
        hera_job_ids = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.submit(spec) for spec in scene_specs])
        logger.info("[%s] hera jobs submitted: %s", job_id, hera_job_ids)

        completed: dict[int, str] = {}
        start_time = asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
        while len(completed) < len(hera_job_ids):
            if asyncio.get_running_loop().time() - start_time > 240:
                raise TimeoutError("Timed out waiting for Hera renders.")
            poll_results = await asyncio.gather(*[hera_client.poll(hid) for hid in hera_job_ids])
            for idx, result in enumerate(poll_results):
                if idx in completed:
                    continue
                status = str(result.get("status", "")).lower()
                file_url = result.get("file_url")
                if status == "failed":
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Hera render {idx} failed: {result.get('error') or 'unknown error'}"
                    )
                if status == "success" and isinstance(file_url, str):
                    completed[idx] = file_url
                    progress = 75 + int((len(completed) / len(hera_job_ids)) * 15)
                    job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.HERA_RENDER, progress=min(progress, 90))
                    logger.info("[%s] clip %d ready (%d/%d)", job_id, idx, len(completed), len(hera_job_ids))
            if len(completed) < len(hera_job_ids):
                await asyncio.sleep(3)

        download_tasks = [
            hera_client.download(completed[idx]) for idx in sorted(completed.keys())
        ]
        clip_bytes_list = await asyncio.gather(*download_tasks)
        clip_paths: list[str] = []
        for index, clip_bytes in enumerate(clip_bytes_list):
            clip_path = out_dir / f"clip_{index}.mp4"
            clip_path.write_bytes(clip_bytes)
            clip_paths.append(str(clip_path))
        job.clip_paths = clip_paths
        logger.info("[%s] all clips downloaded", job_id)

        job_store.update_step(job_id, step=JobStep.COMPOSE, progress=92)
        logger.info("[%s] composing final video", job_id)
        final_video_path = out_dir / "final.mp4"
        compose(clip_paths=clip_paths, audio_path=str(audio_path), out_path=str(final_video_path))
        job_store.set_done(job_id, video_url=f"/jobs/{job_id}/video")
        logger.info("[%s] pipeline complete", job_id)
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.exception("[%s] pipeline failed: %s", job_id, exc)
        job_store.set_error(job_id, str(exc))

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


def compose(
    *,
    intro_clip_path: str,
    intro_sound_path: str,
    scene_clip_paths: list[str],
    voice_path: str,
    out_path: str,
) -> None:
    """Stitch [intro_clip + scene_clips...] video with [intro_sound + voice]
    audio into one MP4. Uses ffmpeg's concat filter (re-encodes), which is
    more lenient than the concat demuxer when input clips have slightly
    different codecs / framerates."""
    if not scene_clip_paths:
        raise ValueError("No scene clip paths provided for composition.")

    out_file = Path(out_path)
    out_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    video_inputs = [intro_clip_path, *scene_clip_paths]
    audio_inputs = [intro_sound_path, voice_path]
    n_video = len(video_inputs)
    n_audio = len(audio_inputs)

    video_chain = "".join(f"[{i}:v]" for i in range(n_video))
    audio_chain = "".join(f"[{n_video + j}:a]" for j in range(n_audio))
    filter_complex = (
        f"{video_chain}concat=n={n_video}:v=1:a=0[v];"
        f"{audio_chain}concat=n={n_audio}:v=0:a=1[a]"
    )

    command: list[str] = ["ffmpeg", "-y"]
    for path in video_inputs:
        command.extend(["-i", path])
    for path in audio_inputs:
        command.extend(["-i", path])
    command.extend(
        [
            "-filter_complex",
            filter_complex,
            "-map",
            "[v]",
            "-map",
            "[a]",
            "-c:v",
            "libx264",
            "-c:a",
            "aac",
            "-shortest",
            "-pix_fmt",
            "yuv420p",
            out_path,
        ]
    )
    subprocess.run(command, check=True, capture_output=True)

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


def compose(clip_paths: list[str], audio_path: str, out_path: str) -> None:
    if not clip_paths:
        raise ValueError("No clip paths provided for composition.")

    out_file = Path(out_path)
    out_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    concat_file = out_file.parent / "concat.txt"
    concat_file.write_text(
        "\n".join(f"file '{Path(clip).as_posix()}'" for clip in clip_paths),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    command = [
        "ffmpeg",
        "-y",
        "-f",
        "concat",
        "-safe",
        "0",
        "-i",
        str(concat_file),
        "-i",
        audio_path,
        "-c:v",
        "libx264",
        "-c:a",
        "aac",
        "-shortest",
        "-pix_fmt",
        "yuv420p",
        out_path,
    ]
    subprocess.run(command, check=True, capture_output=True)

from app.integrations.elevenlabs import compute_slide_chunks_for_scene
from app.agents.hera import build_intro_hera_spec, validate_hera_spec, _normalize_hera_spec
from app.schemas import SlideChunk
from app.pipeline import run_pipeline  # import smoke
from app.agents.finance import run_finance_agent  # smoke
from app.agents.scripter import run_scripter_agent  # smoke
from app.render import compose  # smoke
print('imports OK')

# slide chunker on a scene-relative slice (audio offset = 5s into the full TTS)
text = 'Revenue grew twelve percent. Services drove the gain. Hardware was flat year over year.'
characters = list(text)
# Assume each char takes 0.05s starting at offset 5.0s (scene starts at 5s in the full TTS)
starts = [5.0 + i * 0.05 for i in range(len(characters))]
ends = [5.0 + (i + 1) * 0.05 for i in range(len(characters))]
chunks = compute_slide_chunks_for_scene(
    characters=characters, char_start_times=starts, char_end_times=ends,
    scene_char_start=0, scene_char_end=len(characters),
)
print(f'chunks: {len(chunks)}')
for c in chunks:
    print(f'  [{c.start_seconds:.2f}s - {c.end_seconds:.2f}s] ({c.char_count}c) {c.text!r}')

# intro spec
intro = build_intro_hera_spec(title='Tesla Q3 2026 Briefing')
validate_hera_spec(intro)
print('intro duration:', intro['duration_seconds'])
print('intro prompt head:', intro['prompt'][:140])
print('OK')
"

content.text:
Done. Summary of the rebuild.

## 1) The Hera skill is now a general guide

`.claude/skills/hera-api/SKILL.md` and the mirror `docs/hera-api.md` no longer mention CorpuScan. Two halves:

- **API mechanics** — endpoints, `x-api-key` auth, request/response shapes, status enum (`in-progress | success | failed`), file URL location (`outputs[i].file_url`), a minimal Python client, a curl smoke-test, and 8 common pitfalls.
- **Prompting playbook** —
  - The `[from <START>s to <END>s] <action>` syntax (the single highest-leverage rule).
  - A worked 8-second example.
  - Why timings must come from a TTS transcript, not the script.
  - A reference Python `slide_chunks(...)` algorithm (~80 chars, break on `.!?` ≥30 chars or whitespace ≥80 chars).
  - Visual-primitive selection table (number → count-up; comparison → bar chart; trend → line chart; etc.).
  - The title-intro pattern (Hera typing-effect prompt + ElevenLabs `/v1/sound-generation` typewriter sound).
  - A submission decision checklist.

## 2) Slicer (no new agent — it's deterministic)

I didn't add another LLM agent. Slide-chunking is pure transformation of the ElevenLabs character-level alignment, so it lives in `backend/app/integrations/elevenlabs.py` as `compute_slide_chunks_for_scene(...)`:

- Walks the per-scene character slice.
- Breaks on terminal punctuation once ≥30 chars accumulated, else on whitespace once ≥80 chars.
- Emits `SlideChunk(text, start_seconds, end_seconds, char_count)` with **scene-relative** timings (this fixes a real bug — previously the Hera Agent was getting absolute audio timings, so scenes 2-4 had timings starting at 30s+ instead of 0s).

`backend/app/schemas.py` gains a `SlideChunk` model.

## 3) Hera Agent rewritten

`backend/app/prompts/hera.yaml`:
- Input is now `slide_chunks` (not sentence_timings).
- Output prompt MUST use `[from Xs to Ys]` brackets, one per chunk, covering `[0s, duration_seconds]` contiguously with 0.10–0.30s cross-fades.
- Includes a worked example showing brand paragraph + bracketed beats with overlap.
- Visual-primitive selection rules baked in (numbers → count-up, comparisons → bar chart, etc.).
- Seven explicit timing rules (T1–T7).

`backend/app/agents/hera.py`:
- `run_hera_agent(scene, slide_chunks_for_scene, gemini_client)` — new signature.
- `_normalize_hera_spec` clamps `duration_seconds = ceil(max(slide_chunk.end))`.
- New `build_intro_hera_spec(title, duration_seconds=4)` — deterministic Hera spec for the title intro: typewriter typing of the title in JetBrains Mono 96px on `#F9FAFB`, blinking `#06B6D4` cursor, hold + fade.

## 4) Title intro + typing sound

`backend/app/integrations/elevenlabs.py` gains `generate_sound_effect(text, duration_seconds, prompt_influence=0.5)` → POST `/v1/sound-generation`.

`backend/app/pipeline.py`:
- During TTS, fans out **voice TTS** and **intro typing sound** in parallel (`asyncio.gather`).
- After the 4 Hera Agent calls, builds a 5th deterministic intro spec and submits **all 5 to Hera in parallel**.
- Polls all 5 until `success`; downloads in order (intro = index 0, scenes = 1-4).

`backend/app/render.py` — `compose(intro_clip_path, intro_sound_path, scene_clip_paths, voice_path, out_path)` — uses ffmpeg's `concat` filter (more lenient on codec mismatches than the demuxer):

```
ffmpeg -y -i intro.mp4 -i clip_0..3.mp4 -i intro_sound.mp3 -i voice.mp3 \
  -filter_complex "[0:v]..[4:v]concat=n=5:v=1:a=0[v]; [5:a][6:a]concat=n=2:v=0:a=1[a]" \
  -map [v] -map [a] -c:v libx264 -c:a aac -shortest -pix_fmt yuv420p out.mp4
```

## Verified
- All modules import.
- Slide chunker produces scene-relative timings starting at 0.0s even when fed an absolute-time alignment.
- Intro builder produces a valid Hera spec (passes `validate_hera_spec`).
- ffmpeg command shape inspected end-to-end.
